methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.missing_values_handling
documentation:
Handling of missing values. Either MeanImputation, Skip or PlugValues.

Type: `Literal["mean_imputation", "skip", "plug_values"]`, defaults to `"mean_imputation"`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> boston = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/gbm_test/BostonHousing.csv")
>>> predictors = boston.columns[:-1]
>>> response = "medv"
>>> boston['chas'] = boston['chas'].asfactor()
>>> boston.insert_missing_values()
>>> train, valid = boston.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> boston_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(missing_values_handling="skip")
>>> boston_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                  y=response,
...                  training_frame=train,
...                  validation_frame=valid)
>>> boston_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.plug_values
documentation:
Plug Values (a single row frame containing values that will be used to impute missing values of the
training/validation frame, use with conjunction missing_values_handling = PlugValues).

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars = cars.drop(0)
>>> means = cars.mean()
>>> means = H2OFrame._expr(ExprNode("mean", cars, True, 0))
>>> glm_means = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(seed=42)
>>> glm_means.train(training_frame=cars, y="cylinders")
>>> glm_plugs1 = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(seed=42,
...                                            missing_values_handling="PlugValues",
...                                            plug_values=means)
>>> glm_plugs1.train(training_frame=cars, y="cylinders")
>>> glm_means.coef() == glm_plugs1.coef()
>>> not_means = 0.1 + (means * 0.5)
>>> glm_plugs2 = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(seed=42,
...                                            missing_values_handling="PlugValues",
...                                            plug_values=not_means)
>>> glm_plugs2.train(training_frame=cars, y="cylinders")
>>> glm_means.coef() != glm_plugs2.coef()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.compute_p_values
documentation:
Request p-values computation, p-values work only with IRLSM solver.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"] = airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"] = airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> airlines_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family='binomial',
...                                              lambda_=0,
...                                              remove_collinear_columns=True,
...                                              compute_p_values=True)
>>> airlines_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                    y=response,
...                    training_frame=train,
...                    validation_frame=valid)
>>> airlines_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.dispersion_parameter_method
documentation:
Method used to estimate the dispersion parameter for Tweedie, Gamma and Negative Binomial only.

Type: `Literal["deviance", "pearson", "ml"]`, defaults to `"pearson"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.init_dispersion_parameter
documentation:
Only used for Tweedie, Gamma and Negative Binomial GLM. Store the initial value of dispersion parameter. If
fix_dispersion_parameter is set, this value will be used in the calculation of p-values.

Type: `float`, defaults to `1.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.remove_collinear_columns
documentation:
In case of linearly dependent columns, remove the dependent columns.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"] = airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"] = airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid = airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> airlines_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family='binomial',
...                                              lambda_=0,
...                                              remove_collinear_columns=True)
>>> airlines_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                    y=response,
...                    training_frame=train,
...                    validation_frame=valid)
>>> airlines_glm.auc()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.intercept
documentation:
Include constant term in the model

Type: `bool`, defaults to `True`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> iris = h2o.import_file("http://h2o-public-test-data.s3.amazonaws.com/smalldata/iris/iris_wheader.csv")
>>> iris['class'] = iris['class'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = iris.columns[:-1]
>>> response = 'class'
>>> train, valid = iris.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> iris_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family='multinomial',
...                                          intercept=True)
>>> iris_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> iris_glm.logloss(valid=True)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.non_negative
documentation:
Restrict coefficients (not intercept) to be non-negative.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"] = airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"] = airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> airlines_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family='binomial',
...                                              non_negative=True)
>>> airlines_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                    y=response,
...                    training_frame=train,
...                    validation_frame=valid)
>>> airlines_glm.auc()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.max_iterations
documentation:
Maximum number of iterations. Value should >=1. A value of 0 is only set when only the model coefficient names
and model coefficient dimensions are needed.

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars["economy_20mpg"] = cars["economy_20mpg"].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","acceleration","year"]
>>> response = "economy_20mpg"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> cars_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family='binomial',
...                                          max_iterations=50)
>>> cars_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.objective_epsilon
documentation:
Converge if objective value changes less than this. Default (of -1.0) indicates: If lambda_search is set to
True the value of objective_epsilon is set to .0001. If the lambda_search is set to False and lambda is equal to
zero, the value of objective_epsilon is set to .000001, for any other value of lambda the default value of
objective_epsilon is set to .0001.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> boston = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/gbm_test/BostonHousing.csv")
>>> predictors = boston.columns[:-1]
>>> response = "medv"
>>> boston['chas'] = boston['chas'].asfactor()
>>> train, valid = boston.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> boston_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(objective_epsilon=1e-3)
>>> boston_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                  y=response,
...                  training_frame=train,
...                  validation_frame=valid)
>>> boston_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.beta_epsilon
documentation:
Converge if beta changes less (using L-infinity norm) than beta esilon. ONLY applies to IRLSM solver.

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0001`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","year"]
>>> response = "acceleration"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> cars_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(beta_epsilon=1e-3)
>>> cars_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.gradient_epsilon
documentation:
Converge if objective changes less (using L-infinity norm) than this, ONLY applies to L-BFGS solver. Default
(of -1.0) indicates: If lambda_search is set to False and lambda is equal to zero, the default value of
gradient_epsilon is equal to .000001, otherwise the default value is .0001. If lambda_search is set to True, the
conditional values above are 1E-8 and 1E-6 respectively.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> boston = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/gbm_test/BostonHousing.csv")
>>> predictors = boston.columns[:-1]
>>> response = "medv"
>>> boston['chas'] = boston['chas'].asfactor()
>>> train, valid = boston.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> boston_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(gradient_epsilon=1e-3)
>>> boston_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                  y=response,
...                  training_frame=train,
...                  validation_frame=valid)
>>> boston_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.link
documentation:
Link function.

Type: `Literal["family_default", "identity", "logit", "log", "inverse", "tweedie", "ologit"]`, defaults to
`"family_default"`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> iris = h2o.import_file("http://h2o-public-test-data.s3.amazonaws.com/smalldata/iris/iris_wheader.csv")
>>> iris['class'] = iris['class'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = iris.columns[:-1]
>>> response = 'class'
>>> train, valid = iris.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> iris_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family='multinomial',
...                                          link='family_default')
>>> iris_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> iris_glm.logloss()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.startval
documentation:
double array to initialize coefficients for GLM. If standardize is true, the standardized coefficients should
be used. Otherwise, use the regular coefficients.

Type: `List[float]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.calc_like
documentation:
if true, will return likelihood function value.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.prior
documentation:
Prior probability for y==1. To be used only for logistic regression iff the data has been sampled and the mean
of response does not reflect reality.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars["economy_20mpg"] = cars["economy_20mpg"].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","acceleration","year"]
>>> response = "economy_20mpg"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> cars_glm1 = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family='binomial', prior=0.5)
>>> cars_glm1.train(x=predictors,
...                 y=response,
...                 training_frame=train,
...                 validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_glm1.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.cold_start
documentation:
Only applicable to multiple alpha/lambda values. If false, build the next model for next set of alpha/lambda
values starting from the values provided by current model. If true will start GLM model from scratch.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.lambda_min_ratio
documentation:
Minimum lambda used in lambda search, specified as a ratio of lambda_max (the smallest lambda that drives all
coefficients to zero). Default indicates: if the number of observations is greater than the number of
variables, then lambda_min_ratio is set to 0.0001; if the number of observations is less than the number of
variables, then lambda_min_ratio is set to 0.01.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> boston = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/gbm_test/BostonHousing.csv")
>>> predictors = boston.columns[:-1]
>>> response = "medv"
>>> boston['chas'] = boston['chas'].asfactor()
>>> train, valid = boston.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> boston_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(lambda_min_ratio=.0001)
>>> boston_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                  y=response,
...                  training_frame=train,
...                  validation_frame=valid)
>>> boston_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.beta_constraints
documentation:
Beta constraints

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","year"]
>>> response = "acceleration"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> n = len(predictors)
>>> constraints = h2o.H2OFrame({'names':predictors,
...                             'lower_bounds': [-1000]*n,
...                             'upper_bounds': [1000]*n,
...                             'beta_given': [1]*n,
...                             'rho': [0.2]*n})
>>> cars_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(standardize=True,
...                                          beta_constraints=constraints)
>>> cars_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.max_active_predictors
documentation:
Maximum number of active predictors during computation. Use as a stopping criterion to prevent expensive model
building with many predictors. Default indicates: If the IRLSM solver is used, the value of
max_active_predictors is set to 5000 otherwise it is set to 100000000.

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> higgs= h2o.import_file("https://h2o-public-test-data.s3.amazonaws.com/smalldata/testng/higgs_train_5k.csv")
>>> predictors = higgs.names
>>> predictors.remove('response')
>>> response = "response"
>>> train, valid = higgs.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> higgs_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family='binomial',
...                                           max_active_predictors=200)
>>> higgs_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                 y=response,
...                 training_frame=train,
...                 validation_frame=valid)
>>> higgs_glm.auc()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.interactions
documentation:
A list of predictor column indices to interact. All pairwise combinations will be computed for the list.

Type: `List[str]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> boston = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/gbm_test/BostonHousing.csv")
>>> predictors = boston.columns[:-1]
>>> response = "medv"
>>> boston['chas'] = boston['chas'].asfactor()
>>> train, valid = boston.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> interactions_list = ['crim', 'dis']
>>> boston_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(interactions=interactions_list)
>>> boston_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                  y=response,
...                  training_frame=train,
...                  validation_frame=valid)
>>> boston_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.interaction_pairs
documentation:
A list of pairwise (first order) column interactions.

Type: `List[tuple]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> df = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> XY = [df.names[i-1] for i in [1,2,3,4,6,8,9,13,17,18,19,31]]
>>> interactions = [XY[i-1] for i in [5,7,9]]
>>> m = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(lambda_search=True,
...                                   family="binomial",
...                                   interactions=interactions)
>>> m.train(x=XY[:len(XY)], y=XY[-1],training_frame=df)
>>> m._model_json['output']['coefficients_table']
>>> coef_m = m._model_json['output']['coefficients_table']
>>> interaction_pairs = [("CRSDepTime", "UniqueCarrier"),
...                      ("CRSDepTime", "Origin"),
...                      ("UniqueCarrier", "Origin")]
>>> mexp = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(lambda_search=True,
...                                      family="binomial",
...                                      interaction_pairs=interaction_pairs)
>>> mexp.train(x=XY[:len(XY)], y=XY[-1],training_frame=df)
>>> mexp._model_json['output']['coefficients_table']
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.obj_reg
documentation:
Likelihood divider in objective value computation, default (of -1.0) will set it to 1/nobs.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> df = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/bigdata/laptop/glm_ordinal_logit/ordinal_multinomial_training_set.csv")
>>> df["C11"] = df["C11"].asfactor()
>>> ordinal_fit = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family="ordinal",
...                                             alpha=1.0,
...                                             lambda_=0.000000001,
...                                             obj_reg=0.00001,
...                                             max_iterations=1000,
...                                             beta_epsilon=1e-8,
...                                             objective_epsilon=1e-10)
>>> ordinal_fit.train(x=list(range(0,10)),
...                   y="C11",
...                   training_frame=df)
>>> ordinal_fit.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.stopping_rounds
documentation:
Early stopping based on convergence of stopping_metric. Stop if simple moving average of length k of the
stopping_metric does not improve for k:=stopping_rounds scoring events (0 to disable)

Type: `int`, defaults to `0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.stopping_metric
documentation:
Metric to use for early stopping (AUTO: logloss for classification, deviance for regression and anomaly_score
for Isolation Forest). Note that custom and custom_increasing can only be used in GBM and DRF with the Python
client.

Type: `Literal["auto", "deviance", "logloss", "mse", "rmse", "mae", "rmsle", "auc", "aucpr", "lift_top_group",
"misclassification", "mean_per_class_error", "custom", "custom_increasing"]`, defaults to `"auto"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.stopping_tolerance
documentation:
Relative tolerance for metric-based stopping criterion (stop if relative improvement is not at least this much)

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.001`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.balance_classes
documentation:
Balance training data class counts via over/under-sampling (for imbalanced data).

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","year"]
>>> response = "acceleration"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> cars_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(balance_classes=True,
...                                          seed=1234)
>>> cars_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.class_sampling_factors
documentation:
Desired over/under-sampling ratios per class (in lexicographic order). If not specified, sampling factors will
be automatically computed to obtain class balance during training. Requires balance_classes.

Type: `List[float]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","year"]
>>> response = "acceleration"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> sample_factors = [1., 0.5, 1., 1., 1., 1., 1.]
>>> cars_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(balance_classes=True,
...                                          class_sampling_factors=sample_factors,
...                                          seed=1234)
>>> cars_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.max_after_balance_size
documentation:
Maximum relative size of the training data after balancing class counts (can be less than 1.0). Requires
balance_classes.

Type: `float`, defaults to `5.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","year"]
>>> response = "acceleration"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> max = .85
>>> cars_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(balance_classes=True,
...                                          max_after_balance_size=max,
...                                          seed=1234)
>>> cars_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.max_confusion_matrix_size
documentation:
[Deprecated] Maximum size (# classes) for confusion matrices to be printed in the Logs.

Type: `int`, defaults to `20`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.max_runtime_secs
documentation:
Maximum allowed runtime in seconds for model training. Use 0 to disable.

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars["economy_20mpg"] = cars["economy_20mpg"].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","acceleration","year"]
>>> response = "economy_20mpg"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8])
>>> cars_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(max_runtime_secs=10,
...                                          seed=1234)
>>> cars_glm.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_glm.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.custom_metric_func
documentation:
Reference to custom evaluation function, format: `language:keyName=funcName`

Type: `str`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.generate_scoring_history
documentation:
If set to true, will generate scoring history for GLM. This may significantly slow down the algo.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.auc_type
documentation:
Set default multinomial AUC type.

Type: `Literal["auto", "none", "macro_ovr", "weighted_ovr", "macro_ovo", "weighted_ovo"]`, defaults to
`"auto"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.dispersion_epsilon
documentation:
If changes in dispersion parameter estimation or loglikelihood value is smaller than dispersion_epsilon, will
break out of the dispersion parameter estimation loop using maximum likelihood.

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0001`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.tweedie_epsilon
documentation:
In estimating tweedie dispersion parameter using maximum likelihood, this is used to choose the lower and upper
indices in the approximating of the infinite series summation.

Type: `float`, defaults to `8e-17`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.max_iterations_dispersion
documentation:
Control the maximum number of iterations in the dispersion parameter estimation loop using maximum likelihood.

Type: `int`, defaults to `3000`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.build_null_model
documentation:
If set, will build a model with only the intercept. Default to false.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.fix_dispersion_parameter
documentation:
Only used for Tweedie, Gamma and Negative Binomial GLM. If set, will use the dispsersion parameter in
init_dispersion_parameter as the standard error and use it to calculate the p-values. Default to false.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.generate_variable_inflation_factors
documentation:
if true, will generate variable inflation factors for numerical predictors. Default to false.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> training_data = h2o.import_file("http://h2o-public-test-data.s3.amazonaws.com/smalldata/glm_test/gamma_dispersion_factor_9_10kRows.csv")
>>> predictors = ['abs.C1.','abs.C2.','abs.C3.','abs.C4.','abs.C5.']
>>> response = 'resp'
>>> vif_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family="gamma",
...                                         lambda_=0,
...                                         generate_variable_inflation_factors=True,
...                                         fold_assignment="modulo",
...                                         nfolds=3,
...                                         keep_cross_validation_models=True)
>>> vif_glm.train(x=predictors, y=response, training_frame=training_data)
>>> vif_glm.get_variable_inflation_factors()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.fix_tweedie_variance_power
documentation:
If true, will fix tweedie variance power value to the value set in tweedie_variance_power.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `True`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.dispersion_learning_rate
documentation:
Dispersion learning rate is only valid for tweedie family dispersion parameter estimation using ml. It must be >
0\. This controls how much the dispersion parameter estimate is to be changed when the calculated loglikelihood
actually decreases with the new dispersion. In this case, instead of setting new dispersion = dispersion +
change, we set new dispersion = dispersion + dispersion_learning_rate * change. Defaults to 0.5.

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.5`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.influence
documentation:
If set to dfbetas will calculate the difference in beta when a datarow is included and excluded in the dataset.

Type: `Literal["dfbetas"]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.gainslift_bins
documentation:
Gains/Lift table number of bins. 0 means disabled.. Default value -1 means automatic binning.

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.linear_constraints
documentation:
Linear constraints: used to specify linear constraints involving more than one coefficients in standard form.
It is only supported for solver IRLSM. It contains four columns: names (strings for coefficient names or
constant), values, types ( strings of 'Equal' or 'LessThanEqual'), constraint_numbers (0 for first linear
constraint, 1 for second linear constraint, ...).

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.init_optimal_glm
documentation:
If true, will initialize coefficients with values derived from GLM runs without linear constraints. Only
available for linear constraints.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.separate_linear_beta
documentation:
If true, will keep the beta constraints and linear constraints separate. After new coefficients are found,
first beta constraints will be applied followed by the application of linear constraints. Note that the beta
constraints in this case will not be part of the objective function. If false, will combine the beta and linear
constraints.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.constraint_eta0
documentation:
For constrained GLM only. It affects the setting of eta_k+1=eta_0/power(ck+1, alpha).

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.1258925`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.constraint_tau
documentation:
For constrained GLM only. It affects the setting of c_k+1=tau\*c_k.

Type: `float`, defaults to `10.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.constraint_alpha
documentation:
For constrained GLM only. It affects the setting of eta_k = eta_0/pow(c_0, alpha).

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.1`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.constraint_beta
documentation:
For constrained GLM only. It affects the setting of eta_k+1 = eta_k/pow(c_k, beta).

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.9`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.constraint_c0
documentation:
For constrained GLM only. It affects the initial setting of epsilon_k = 1/c_0.

Type: `float`, defaults to `10.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.get_regression_influence_diagnostics
documentation:
For GLM model, if influence is set to dfbetas, a frame containing the original predictors, response
and [DFBETA] for each predictors that are used in building the model is returned.

```
return

H2OFrame containing predictors used in building the model, response and DFBETA for each predictor.

examples

```

```pycon
>>> d = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/prostate/prostate.csv")
>>> m = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family = 'binomial',
...                                   lambda_=0.0,
...                                   standardize=False,
...                                   influence="dfbetas")
>>> m.train(training_frame = d,
...         x = [2,3,4,5,6,7,8],
...         y = 1)
>>> ridFrame = m.get_regression_influence_diagnostics()
>>> print("column names of regression influence diagnostics frame is {0}".format(ridFrame.names))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getAlphaBest
documentation:
Extract best alpha value found from glm model.

```
param model

source lambda search model

examples

```

```pycon
>>> d = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/prostate/prostate.csv")
>>> m = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family = 'binomial',
...                                   lambda_search = True,
...                                   solver = 'COORDINATE_DESCENT')
>>> m.train(training_frame = d,
...         x = [2,3,4,5,6,7,8],
...         y = 1)
>>> bestAlpha = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getAlphaBest(m)
>>> print("Best alpha found is {0}".format(bestAlpha))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getLambdaBest
documentation:
Extract best lambda value found from glm model.

```
param model

source lambda search model

examples

```

```pycon
>>> d = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/prostate/prostate.csv")
>>> m = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family = 'binomial',
...                                   lambda_search = True,
...                                   solver = 'COORDINATE_DESCENT')
>>> m.train(training_frame = d,
...         x = [2,3,4,5,6,7,8],
...         y = 1)
>>> bestLambda = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getLambdaBest(m)
>>> print("Best lambda found is {0}".format(bestLambda))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getLambdaMax
documentation:
Extract the maximum lambda value used during lambda search.

```
param model

source lambda search model

examples

```

```pycon
>>> d = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/prostate/prostate.csv")
>>> m = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family = 'binomial',
...                                   lambda_search = True,
...                                   solver = 'COORDINATE_DESCENT')
>>> m.train(training_frame = d,
...         x = [2,3,4,5,6,7,8],
...         y = 1)
>>> maxLambda = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getLambdaMax(m)
>>> print("Maximum lambda found is {0}".format(maxLambda))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getLambdaMin
documentation:
Extract the minimum lambda value calculated during lambda search from glm model. Note that due to early stop,
this minimum lambda value may not be used in the actual lambda search.

```
param model

source lambda search model

examples

```

```pycon
>>> d = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/prostate/prostate.csv")
>>> m = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family = 'binomial',
...                                   lambda_search = True,
...                                   solver = 'COORDINATE_DESCENT')
>>> m.train(training_frame = d,
...         x = [2,3,4,5,6,7,8],
...         y = 1)
>>> minLambda = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getLambdaMin(m)
>>> print("Minimum lambda found is {0}".format(minLambda))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getGLMRegularizationPath
documentation:
Extract full regularization path explored during lambda search from glm model.

```
param model

source lambda search model

examples

```

```pycon
>>> d = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/prostate/prostate.csv")
>>> m = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family = 'binomial',
...                                   lambda_search = True,
...                                   solver = 'COORDINATE_DESCENT')
>>> m.train(training_frame = d,
...         x = [2,3,4,5,6,7,8],
...         y = 1)
>>> r = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getGLMRegularizationPath(m)
>>> m2 = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.makeGLMModel(model=m,
...                                                 coefs=r['coefficients'][10])
>>> dev1 = r['explained_deviance_train'][10]
>>> p = m2.model_performance(d)
>>> dev2 = 1-p.residual_deviance()/p.null_deviance()
>>> print(dev1, " =?= ", dev2)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.makeGLMModel
documentation:
Create a custom GLM model using the given coefficients.

Needs to be passed source model trained on the dataset to extract the dataset information from.

```
param model

source model, used for extracting dataset information

param coefs

dictionary containing model coefficients

param threshold

(optional, only for binomial) decision threshold used for classification

examples

```

```pycon
>>> d = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/prostate/prostate.csv")
>>> m = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family='binomial',
...                                   lambda_search=True,
...                                   solver='COORDINATE_DESCENT')
>>> m.train(training_frame=d,
...         x=[2,3,4,5,6,7,8],
...         y=1)
>>> r = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getGLMRegularizationPath(m)
>>> m2 = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.makeGLMModel(model=m,
...                                                 coefs=r['coefficients'][10])
>>> dev1 = r['explained_deviance_train'][10]
>>> p = m2.model_performance(d)
>>> dev2 = 1-p.residual_deviance()/p.null_deviance()
>>> print(dev1, " =?= ", dev2)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getConstraintsInfo
documentation:
Given a constrained GLM model, the constraints descriptions, constraints values, constraints conditions and
whether the constraints are satisfied (true) or not (false) are returned.

```
param model

GLM model with linear and beta (if applicable)  constraints

return

H2OTwoDimTable containing the above constraints information.

example

```

```pycon
>>> train = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/glm_test/binomial_20_cols_10KRows.csv")
>>> response = "C21"
>>> predictors = list(range(0,20))
>>> loose_init_const = [] # this constraint is satisfied by default coefficient initialization
>>> # add loose constraints
>>> name = "C19"
>>> values = 0.5
>>> types = "LessThanEqual"
>>> contraint_numbers = 0
>>> loose_init_const.append([name, values, types, contraint_numbers])
>>> name = "C20"
>>> values = -0.8
>>> types = "LessThanEqual"
>>> contraint_numbers = 0
>>> loose_init_const.append([name, values, types, contraint_numbers])
>>> name = "constant"
>>> values = -1000
>>> types = "LessThanEqual"
>>> contraint_numbers = 0
>>> loose_init_const.append([name, values, types, contraint_numbers])
>>> linear_constraints2 = h2o.H2OFrame(loose_init_const)
>>> linear_constraints2.set_names(["names", "values", "types", "constraint_numbers"])
>>> # GLM model with GLM coefficients with default initialization
>>> h2o_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family="binomial", compute_p_values=True, remove_collinear_columns=True,
...                                         lambda_=0.0, solver="irlsm", linear_constraints=linear_constraints2,
...                                         init_optimal_glm = False, seed=12345)
>>> h2o_glm.train(x=predictors, y=response, training_frame=train)
>>> print(H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.getConstraintsInfo(h2o_glm))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.glm.H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.allConstraintsPassed
documentation:
Given a constrainted GLM model, this will return true if all beta (if exists) and linear constraints are

: satified. It will return false even if one constraint is not satisfied. To see which ones failed, use
  getConstraintsInfo function.

```
param model

GLM model with linear and beta (if applicable)  constraints

return

boolean True or False

example

```

```pycon
>>> train = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/glm_test/binomial_20_cols_10KRows.csv")
>>> response = "C21"
>>> predictors = list(range(0,20))
>>> loose_init_const = [] # this constraint is satisfied by default coefficient initialization
>>> # add loose constraints
>>> name = "C19"
>>> values = 0.5
>>> types = "LessThanEqual"
>>> contraint_numbers = 0
>>> loose_init_const.append([name, values, types, contraint_numbers])
>>> name = "C20"
>>> values = -0.8
>>> types = "LessThanEqual"
>>> contraint_numbers = 0
>>> loose_init_const.append([name, values, types, contraint_numbers])
>>> name = "constant"
>>> values = -1000
>>> types = "LessThanEqual"
>>> contraint_numbers = 0
>>> loose_init_const.append([name, values, types, contraint_numbers])
>>> linear_constraints2 = h2o.H2OFrame(loose_init_const)
>>> linear_constraints2.set_names(["names", "values", "types", "constraint_numbers"])
>>> # GLM model with GLM coefficients with default initialization
>>> h2o_glm = H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator(family="binomial", compute_p_values=True, remove_collinear_columns=True,
...                                         lambda_=0.0, solver="irlsm", linear_constraints=linear_constraints2,
...                                         init_optimal_glm = False, seed=12345)
>>> h2o_glm.train(x=predictors, y=response, training_frame=train)
>>> print(H2OGeneralizedLinearEstimator.allConstraintsPassed(h2o_glm))
```

Generalized Linear Modeling

Fits a generalized linear model, specified by a response variable, a set of predictors, and a
description of the error distribution.

A subclass of {class}`ModelBase` is returned. The specific subclass depends on the machine learning task
at hand (if it's binomial classification, then an H2OBinomialModel is returned, if it's regression then a
H2ORegressionModel is returned). The default print-out of the models is shown, but further GLM-specific
information can be queried out of the object. Upon completion of the GLM, the resulting object has
coefficients, normalized coefficients, residual/null deviance, aic, and a host of model metrics including
MSE, AUC (for logistic regression), degrees of freedom, and confusion matrices.

classes.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.__init__
documentation:
---
'param ': |-
  Regularization strength
  Defaults to `None`.
param alpha: |-
  Distribution of regularization between the L1 (Lasso) and L2 (Ridge) penalties. A value of 1 for
  alpha represents Lasso regression, a value of 0 produces Ridge regression, and anything in between
  specifies the amount of mixing between the two. Default value of alpha is 0 when SOLVER = 'L-BFGS'; 0.5
  otherwise.
  Defaults to `None`.
param auc_type: |-
  Set default multinomial AUC type.
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param balance_classes: |-
  Balance training data class counts via over/under-sampling (for imbalanced data).
  Defaults to `False`.
param beta_constraints: |-
  Beta constraints
  Defaults to `None`.
param beta_epsilon: |-
  Converge if beta changes less (using L-infinity norm) than beta esilon, ONLY applies to
  IRLSM solver
  Defaults to `0.0001`.
param bs: |-
  Basis function type for each gam predictors, 0 for cr, 1 for thin plate regression with knots, 2 for
  monotone I-splines, 3 for NBSplineTypeI M-splines (refer to doc here:
  <https://github.com/h2oai/h2o-3/issues/6926>). If specified, must be the same size as gam_columns
  Defaults to `None`.
param class_sampling_factors: |-
  Desired over/under-sampling ratios per class (in lexicographic order). If not
  specified, sampling factors will be automatically computed to obtain class balance during training.
  Requires balance_classes.
  Defaults to `None`.
param cold_start: |-
  Only applicable to multiple alpha/lambda values when calling GLM from GAM. If false, build
  the next model for next set of alpha/lambda values starting from the values provided by current model.
  If true will start GLM model from scratch.
  Defaults to `False`.
param compute_p_values: |-
  Request p-values computation, p-values work only with IRLSM solver and no
  regularization
  Defaults to `False`.
param early_stopping: |-
  Stop early when there is no more relative improvement on train or validation (if
  provided)
  Defaults to `True`.
param export_checkpoints_dir: |-
  Automatically export generated models to this directory.
  Defaults to `None`.
param family: |-
  Family. Use binomial for classification with logistic regression, others are for regression
  problems.
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param fold_assignment: |-
  Cross-validation fold assignment scheme, if fold_column is not specified. The
  'Stratified' option will stratify the folds based on the response variable, for classification problems.
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param fold_column: |-
  Column with cross-validation fold index assignment per observation.
  Defaults to `None`.
param gainslift_bins: |-
  Gains/Lift table number of bins. 0 means disabled.. Default value -1 means automatic
  binning.
  Defaults to `-1`.
param gam_columns: |-
  Arrays of predictor column names for gam for smoothers using single or multiple predictors
  like {{'c1'},{'c2','c3'},{'c4'},...}
  Defaults to `None`.
param gradient_epsilon: |-
  Converge if objective changes less (using L-infinity norm) than this, ONLY applies to
  L-BFGS solver. Default indicates: If lambda_search is set to False and lambda is equal to zero, the
  default value of gradient_epsilon is equal to .000001, otherwise the default value is .0001. If
  lambda_search is set to True, the conditional values above are 1E-8 and 1E-6 respectively.
  Defaults to `-1.0`.
param ignore_const_cols: |-
  Ignore constant columns.
  Defaults to `True`.
param ignored_columns: |-
  Names of columns to ignore for training.
  Defaults to `None`.
param interaction_pairs: |-
  A list of pairwise (first order) column interactions.
  Defaults to `None`.
param interactions: |-
  A list of predictor column indices to interact. All pairwise combinations will be computed
  for the list.
  Defaults to `None`.
param intercept: |-
  Include constant term in the model
  Defaults to `True`.
param keep_cross_validation_fold_assignment: |-
  Whether to keep the cross-validation fold assignment.
  Defaults to `False`.
param keep_cross_validation_models: |-
  Whether to keep the cross-validation models.
  Defaults to `True`.
param keep_cross_validation_predictions: |-
  Whether to keep the predictions of the cross-validation models.
  Defaults to `False`.
param keep_gam_cols: |-
  Save keys of model matrix
  Defaults to `False`.
param knot_ids: |-
  Array storing frame keys of knots. One for each gam column set specified in gam_columns
  Defaults to `None`.
param lambda_min_ratio: |-
  Minimum lambda used in lambda search, specified as a ratio of lambda_max (the smallest
  lambda that drives all coefficients to zero). Default indicates: if the number of observations is greater
  than the number of variables, then lambda_min_ratio is set to 0.0001; if the number of observations is
  less than the number of variables, then lambda_min_ratio is set to 0.01.
  Defaults to `-1.0`.
param lambda_search: |-
  Use lambda search starting at lambda max, given lambda is then interpreted as lambda min
  Defaults to `False`.
param link: |-
  Link function.
  Defaults to `"family_default"`.
param max_active_predictors: |-
  Maximum number of active predictors during computation. Use as a stopping
  criterion to prevent expensive model building with many predictors. Default indicates: If the IRLSM
  solver is used, the value of max_active_predictors is set to 5000 otherwise it is set to 100000000.
  Defaults to `-1`.
param max_after_balance_size: |-
  Maximum relative size of the training data after balancing class counts (can be
  less than 1.0). Requires balance_classes.
  Defaults to `5.0`.
param max_confusion_matrix_size: |-
  [Deprecated] Maximum size (# classes) for confusion matrices to be printed in
  the Logs
  Defaults to `20`.
param max_iterations: |-
  Maximum number of iterations
  Defaults to `-1`.
param max_runtime_secs: |-
  Maximum allowed runtime in seconds for model training. Use 0 to disable.
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param missing_values_handling: |-
  Handling of missing values. Either MeanImputation, Skip or PlugValues.
  Defaults to `"mean_imputation"`.
param model_id: |-
  Destination id for this model; auto-generated if not specified.
  Defaults to `None`.
param nfolds: |-
  Number of folds for K-fold cross-validation (0 to disable or >= 2).
  Defaults to `0`.
param nlambdas: |-
  Number of lambdas to be used in a search. Default indicates: If alpha is zero, with lambda
  search set to True, the value of nlamdas is set to 30 (fewer lambdas are needed for ridge regression)
  otherwise it is set to 100.
  Defaults to `-1`.
param non_negative: |-
  Restrict coefficients (not intercept) to be non-negative
  Defaults to `False`.
param num_knots: |-
  Number of knots for gam predictors. If specified, must specify one for each gam predictor.
  For monotone I-splines, mininum = 2, for cs spline, minimum = 3. For thin plate, minimum is size of
  polynomial basis + 2.
  Defaults to `None`.
param obj_reg: |-
  Likelihood divider in objective value computation, default is 1/nobs
  Defaults to `-1.0`.
param objective_epsilon: |-
  Converge if objective value changes less than this. Default indicates: If
  lambda_search is set to True the value of objective_epsilon is set to .0001. If the lambda_search is set
  to False and lambda is equal to zero, the value of objective_epsilon is set to .000001, for any other
  value of lambda the default value of objective_epsilon is set to .0001.
  Defaults to `-1.0`.
param offset_column: |-
  Offset column. This will be added to the combination of columns before applying the link
  function.
  Defaults to `None`.
param plug_values: |-
  Plug Values (a single row frame containing values that will be used to impute missing values
  of the training/validation frame, use with conjunction missing_values_handling = PlugValues)
  Defaults to `None`.
param prior: |-
  Prior probability for y==1. To be used only for logistic regression iff the data has been sampled
  and the mean of response does not reflect reality.
  Defaults to `-1.0`.
param remove_collinear_columns: |-
  In case of linearly dependent columns, remove some of the dependent columns
  Defaults to `False`.
param response_column: |-
  Response variable column.
  Defaults to `None`.
param scale: |-
  Smoothing parameter for gam predictors. If specified, must be of the same length as gam_columns
  Defaults to `None`.
param scale_tp_penalty_mat: |-
  Scale penalty matrix for tp (thin plate) smoothers as in R
  Defaults to `False`.
param score_each_iteration: |-
  Whether to score during each iteration of model training.
  Defaults to `False`.
param seed: |-
  Seed for pseudo random number generator (if applicable)
  Defaults to `-1`.
param solver: |-
  AUTO will set the solver based on given data and the other parameters. IRLSM is fast on on
  problems with small number of predictors and for lambda-search with L1 penalty, L_BFGS scales better for
  datasets with many columns.
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param spline_orders: |-
  Order of I-splines or NBSplineTypeI M-splines used for gam predictors. If specified, must
  be the same size as gam_columns. For I-splines, the spline_orders will be the same as the polynomials
  used to generate the splines. For M-splines, the polynomials used to generate the splines will be
  spline_order-1. Values for bs=0 or 1 will be ignored.
  Defaults to `None`.
param splines_non_negative: |-
  Valid for I-spline (bs=2) only. True if the I-splines are monotonically increasing
  (and monotonically non-decreasing) and False if the I-splines are monotonically decreasing (and
  monotonically non-increasing). If specified, must be the same size as gam_columns. Values for other
  spline types will be ignored. Default to true.
  Defaults to `None`.
param standardize: |-
  Standardize numeric columns to have zero mean and unit variance
  Defaults to `False`.
param standardize_tp_gam_cols: |-
  standardize tp (thin plate) predictor columns
  Defaults to `False`.
param startval: |-
  double array to initialize coefficients for GAM.
  Defaults to `None`.
param stopping_metric: |-
  Metric to use for early stopping (AUTO: logloss for classification, deviance for
  regression and anomaly_score for Isolation Forest). Note that custom and custom_increasing can only be
  used in GBM and DRF with the Python client.
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param stopping_rounds: |-
  Early stopping based on convergence of stopping_metric. Stop if simple moving average of
  length k of the stopping_metric does not improve for k:=stopping_rounds scoring events (0 to disable)
  Defaults to `0`.
param stopping_tolerance: |-
  Relative tolerance for metric-based stopping criterion (stop if relative improvement
  is not at least this much)
  Defaults to `0.001`.
param store_knot_locations: |-
  If set to true, will return knot locations as double[][] array for gam column names
  found knots_for_gam. Default to false.
  Defaults to `False`.
param theta: |-
  Theta
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param training_frame: |-
  Id of the training data frame.
  Defaults to `None`.
param tweedie_link_power: |-
  Tweedie link power
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param tweedie_variance_power: |-
  Tweedie variance power
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param validation_frame: |-
  Id of the validation data frame.
  Defaults to `None`.
param weights_column: |-
  Column with observation weights. Giving some observation a weight of zero is equivalent
  to excluding it from the dataset; giving an observation a relative weight of 2 is equivalent to repeating
  that row twice. Negative weights are not allowed. Note: Weights are per-row observation weights and do
  not increase the size of the data frame. This is typically the number of times a row is repeated, but
  non-integer values are supported as well. During training, rows with higher weights matter more, due to
  the larger loss function pre-factor. If you set weight = 0 for a row, the returned prediction frame at
  that row is zero and this is incorrect. To get an accurate prediction, remove all rows with weight == 0.
  Defaults to `None`.
'type ': List[float], optional
type alpha: List[float], optional
type auc_type: Literal["auto", "none", "macro_ovr", "weighted_ovr", "macro_ovo", "weighted_ovo"]
type balance_classes: bool

---

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.training_frame
documentation:
Id of the training data frame.

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.validation_frame
documentation:
Id of the validation data frame.

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.nfolds
documentation:
Number of folds for K-fold cross-validation (0 to disable or >= 2).

Type: `int`, defaults to `0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.seed
documentation:
Seed for pseudo random number generator (if applicable)

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.keep_cross_validation_models
documentation:
Whether to keep the cross-validation models.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `True`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.keep_cross_validation_predictions
documentation:
Whether to keep the predictions of the cross-validation models.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.keep_cross_validation_fold_assignment
documentation:
Whether to keep the cross-validation fold assignment.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.fold_assignment
documentation:
Cross-validation fold assignment scheme, if fold_column is not specified. The 'Stratified' option will stratify
the folds based on the response variable, for classification problems.

Type: `Literal["auto", "random", "modulo", "stratified"]`, defaults to `"auto"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.fold_column
documentation:
Column with cross-validation fold index assignment per observation.

Type: `str`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.response_column
documentation:
Response variable column.

Type: `str`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.ignored_columns
documentation:
Names of columns to ignore for training.

Type: `List[str]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.ignore_const_cols
documentation:
Ignore constant columns.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `True`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.score_each_iteration
documentation:
Whether to score during each iteration of model training.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.offset_column
documentation:
Offset column. This will be added to the combination of columns before applying the link function.

Type: `str`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.weights_column
documentation:
Column with observation weights. Giving some observation a weight of zero is equivalent to excluding it from the
dataset; giving an observation a relative weight of 2 is equivalent to repeating that row twice. Negative
weights are not allowed. Note: Weights are per-row observation weights and do not increase the size of the data
frame. This is typically the number of times a row is repeated, but non-integer values are supported as well.
During training, rows with higher weights matter more, due to the larger loss function pre-factor. If you set
weight = 0 for a row, the returned prediction frame at that row is zero and this is incorrect. To get an
accurate prediction, remove all rows with weight == 0.

Type: `str`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.family
documentation:
Family. Use binomial for classification with logistic regression, others are for regression problems.

Type: `Literal["auto", "gaussian", "binomial", "quasibinomial", "ordinal", "multinomial", "poisson", "gamma",
"tweedie", "negativebinomial", "fractionalbinomial"]`, defaults to `"auto"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.tweedie_variance_power
documentation:
Tweedie variance power

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.tweedie_link_power
documentation:
Tweedie link power

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.theta
documentation:
Theta

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.solver
documentation:
AUTO will set the solver based on given data and the other parameters. IRLSM is fast on on problems with small
number of predictors and for lambda-search with L1 penalty, L_BFGS scales better for datasets with many columns.

Type: `Literal["auto", "irlsm", "l_bfgs", "coordinate_descent_naive", "coordinate_descent",
"gradient_descent_lh", "gradient_descent_sqerr"]`, defaults to `"auto"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.alpha
documentation:
Distribution of regularization between the L1 (Lasso) and L2 (Ridge) penalties. A value of 1 for alpha
represents Lasso regression, a value of 0 produces Ridge regression, and anything in between specifies the
amount of mixing between the two. Default value of alpha is 0 when SOLVER = 'L-BFGS'; 0.5 otherwise.

Type: `List[float]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.lambda_
documentation:
Regularization strength

Type: `List[float]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.lambda_search
documentation:
Use lambda search starting at lambda max, given lambda is then interpreted as lambda min

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.early_stopping
documentation:
Stop early when there is no more relative improvement on train or validation (if provided)

Type: `bool`, defaults to `True`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.nlambdas
documentation:
Number of lambdas to be used in a search. Default indicates: If alpha is zero, with lambda search set to True,
the value of nlamdas is set to 30 (fewer lambdas are needed for ridge regression) otherwise it is set to 100.

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.standardize
documentation:
Standardize numeric columns to have zero mean and unit variance

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.missing_values_handling
documentation:
Handling of missing values. Either MeanImputation, Skip or PlugValues.

Type: `Literal["mean_imputation", "skip", "plug_values"]`, defaults to `"mean_imputation"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.plug_values
documentation:
Plug Values (a single row frame containing values that will be used to impute missing values of the
training/validation frame, use with conjunction missing_values_handling = PlugValues)

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.compute_p_values
documentation:
Request p-values computation, p-values work only with IRLSM solver and no regularization

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.remove_collinear_columns
documentation:
In case of linearly dependent columns, remove some of the dependent columns

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.splines_non_negative
documentation:
Valid for I-spline (bs=2) only. True if the I-splines are monotonically increasing (and monotonically non-
decreasing) and False if the I-splines are monotonically decreasing (and monotonically non-increasing). If
specified, must be the same size as gam_columns. Values for other spline types will be ignored. Default to
true.

Type: `List[bool]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators.gam import H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> h2o_data = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/glm_test/binomial_20_cols_10KRows.csv")
>>> y = "C21"
>>> x = ["C19","C20"]
>>> numKnots = [5,5,5]
>>> h2o_model = H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator(family='gaussian',
...                                             gam_columns=["C16","C17","C18"],
...                                             bs=[2,2,2],
...                                             splines_non_negative=[True, True, True])
>>> h2o_model.train(x=x, y=y, training_frame=h2o_data)
>>> h2o_model.coef()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.intercept
documentation:
Include constant term in the model

Type: `bool`, defaults to `True`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.non_negative
documentation:
Restrict coefficients (not intercept) to be non-negative

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.max_iterations
documentation:
Maximum number of iterations

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.objective_epsilon
documentation:
Converge if objective value changes less than this. Default indicates: If lambda_search is set to True the
value of objective_epsilon is set to .0001. If the lambda_search is set to False and lambda is equal to zero,
the value of objective_epsilon is set to .000001, for any other value of lambda the default value of
objective_epsilon is set to .0001.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.beta_epsilon
documentation:
Converge if beta changes less (using L-infinity norm) than beta esilon, ONLY applies to IRLSM solver

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0001`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.gradient_epsilon
documentation:
Converge if objective changes less (using L-infinity norm) than this, ONLY applies to L-BFGS solver. Default
indicates: If lambda_search is set to False and lambda is equal to zero, the default value of gradient_epsilon
is equal to .000001, otherwise the default value is .0001. If lambda_search is set to True, the conditional
values above are 1E-8 and 1E-6 respectively.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.link
documentation:
Link function.

Type: `Literal["family_default", "identity", "logit", "log", "inverse", "tweedie", "ologit"]`, defaults to
`"family_default"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.startval
documentation:
double array to initialize coefficients for GAM.

Type: `List[float]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.prior
documentation:
Prior probability for y==1. To be used only for logistic regression iff the data has been sampled and the mean
of response does not reflect reality.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.cold_start
documentation:
Only applicable to multiple alpha/lambda values when calling GLM from GAM. If false, build the next model for
next set of alpha/lambda values starting from the values provided by current model. If true will start GLM
model from scratch.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.lambda_min_ratio
documentation:
Minimum lambda used in lambda search, specified as a ratio of lambda_max (the smallest lambda that drives all
coefficients to zero). Default indicates: if the number of observations is greater than the number of variables,
then lambda_min_ratio is set to 0.0001; if the number of observations is less than the number of variables, then
lambda_min_ratio is set to 0.01.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.beta_constraints
documentation:
Beta constraints

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.max_active_predictors
documentation:
Maximum number of active predictors during computation. Use as a stopping criterion to prevent expensive model
building with many predictors. Default indicates: If the IRLSM solver is used, the value of
max_active_predictors is set to 5000 otherwise it is set to 100000000.

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.interactions
documentation:
A list of predictor column indices to interact. All pairwise combinations will be computed for the list.

Type: `List[str]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.interaction_pairs
documentation:
A list of pairwise (first order) column interactions.

Type: `List[tuple]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.obj_reg
documentation:
Likelihood divider in objective value computation, default is 1/nobs

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.export_checkpoints_dir
documentation:
Automatically export generated models to this directory.

Type: `str`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.stopping_rounds
documentation:
Early stopping based on convergence of stopping_metric. Stop if simple moving average of length k of the
stopping_metric does not improve for k:=stopping_rounds scoring events (0 to disable)

Type: `int`, defaults to `0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.stopping_metric
documentation:
Metric to use for early stopping (AUTO: logloss for classification, deviance for regression and anomaly_score
for Isolation Forest). Note that custom and custom_increasing can only be used in GBM and DRF with the Python
client.

Type: `Literal["auto", "deviance", "logloss", "mse", "rmse", "mae", "rmsle", "auc", "aucpr", "lift_top_group",
"misclassification", "mean_per_class_error", "custom", "custom_increasing"]`, defaults to `"auto"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.stopping_tolerance
documentation:
Relative tolerance for metric-based stopping criterion (stop if relative improvement is not at least this much)

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.001`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.balance_classes
documentation:
Balance training data class counts via over/under-sampling (for imbalanced data).

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.class_sampling_factors
documentation:
Desired over/under-sampling ratios per class (in lexicographic order). If not specified, sampling factors will
be automatically computed to obtain class balance during training. Requires balance_classes.

Type: `List[float]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.max_after_balance_size
documentation:
Maximum relative size of the training data after balancing class counts (can be less than 1.0). Requires
balance_classes.

Type: `float`, defaults to `5.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.max_confusion_matrix_size
documentation:
[Deprecated] Maximum size (# classes) for confusion matrices to be printed in the Logs

Type: `int`, defaults to `20`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.max_runtime_secs
documentation:
Maximum allowed runtime in seconds for model training. Use 0 to disable.

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.num_knots
documentation:
Number of knots for gam predictors. If specified, must specify one for each gam predictor. For monotone
I-splines, mininum = 2, for cs spline, minimum = 3. For thin plate, minimum is size of polynomial basis + 2.

Type: `List[int]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators.gam import H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> h2o_data = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/glm_test/multinomial_10_classes_10_cols_10000_Rows_train.csv")
>>> h2o_data["C11"] = h2o_data["C11"].asfactor()
>>> train, test = h2o_data.split_frame(ratios = [.8])
>>> y = "C11"
>>> x = ["C9","C10"]
>>> h2o_model = H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator(family='multinomial',
...                                             store_knot_locations=True,
...                                             gam_columns=["C6","C7","C8"],
...                                             num_knots=[3,4,5])
>>> h2o_model.train(x=x, y=y, training_frame=h2o_data)
>>> h2o_model.get_knot_locations()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.spline_orders
documentation:
Order of I-splines or NBSplineTypeI M-splines used for gam predictors. If specified, must be the same size as
gam_columns. For I-splines, the spline_orders will be the same as the polynomials used to generate the splines.
For M-splines, the polynomials used to generate the splines will be spline_order-1. Values for bs=0 or 1 will
be ignored.

Type: `List[int]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators.gam import H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> h2o_data = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/glm_test/binomial_20_cols_10KRows.csv")
>>> y = "C21"
>>> x = ["C19","C20"]
>>> numKnots = [5,5,5]
>>> h2o_model = H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator(family='gaussian',
...                                             gam_columns=["C16","C17","C18"],
...                                             bs=[2,2,2],
...                                             spline_orders=[3,4,5])
>>> h2o_model.train(x=x, y=y, training_frame=h2o_data)
>>> h2o_model.coef()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.knot_ids
documentation:
Array storing frame keys of knots. One for each gam column set specified in gam_columns

Type: `List[str]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators.gam import H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> knots1 = [-1.99905699, -0.98143075, 0.02599159, 1.00770987, 1.99942290]
>>> frameKnots1 = h2o.H2OFrame(python_obj=knots1)
>>> knots2 = [-1.999821861, -1.005257990, -0.006716042, 1.002197392, 1.999073589]
>>> frameKnots2 = h2o.H2OFrame(python_obj=knots2)
>>> knots3 = [-1.999675688, -0.979893796, 0.007573327, 1.011437347, 1.999611676]
>>> frameKnots3 = h2o.H2OFrame(python_obj=knots3)
>>> h2o_data = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/glm_test/multinomial_10_classes_10_cols_10000_Rows_train.csv")()
>>> h2o_data["C11"] = h2o_data["C11"].asfactor()
>>> train, test = h2o_data.split_frame(ratios = [.8])
>>> y = "C11"
>>> x = ["C9","C10"]
>>> h2o_model = H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator(family='multinomial',
...                                             gam_columns=["C6","C7","C8"],
...                                             store_knot_locations=True,
...                                             knot_ids=[frameKnots1.key, frameKnots2.key, frameKnots3.key])
>>> h2o_model.train(x=x, y=y, training_frame=h2o_data)
>>> h2o_model.get_knot_locations()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.gam_columns
documentation:
Arrays of predictor column names for gam for smoothers using single or multiple predictors like
{{'c1'},{'c2','c3'},{'c4'},...}

Type: `List[List[str]]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators.gam import H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator
>>> h2o.init()

documentation:
Retrieve gam columns knot locations if store_knot_locations parameter is enabled. If a gam column name is

returned for all gam columns. The order of the gam columns are specified in gam_knot_column_names of the
model output.

```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.gam.H2OGeneralizedAdditiveEstimator.get_gam_knot_column_names
documentation:
Retrieve gam column names corresponding to the knot locations that will be returned if store_knot_locations
parameter is enabled.

```
return

gam column names whose knot locations are stored in the knot_locations.
```

Generalized Additive Model

Fits a generalized additive model, specified by a response variable, a set of predictors, and a
description of the error distribution.

A subclass of {class}`ModelBase` is returned. The specific subclass depends on the machine learning task
at hand (if it's binomial classification, then an H2OBinomialModel is returned, if it's regression then a
H2ORegressionModel is returned). The default print-out of the models is shown, but further GAM-specific
information can be queried out of the object. Upon completion of the GAM, the resulting object has
coefficients, normalized coefficients, residual/null deviance, aic, and a host of model metrics including
MSE, AUC (for logistic regression), degrees of freedom, and confusion matrices.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.isolation_forest.H2OIsolationForestEstimator.validation_response_column
documentation:
(experimental) Name of the response column in the validation frame. Response column should be binary and
indicate not anomaly/anomaly.

Type: `str`.

Isolation Forest

Builds an Isolation Forest model. Isolation Forest algorithm samples the training frame
and in each iteration builds a tree that partitions the space of the sample observations until
it isolates each observation. Length of the path from root to a leaf node of the resulting tree
is used to calculate the anomaly score. Anomalies are easier to isolate and their average
tree path is expected to be shorter than paths of regular observations.

Whether to score during each iteration of model training.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> data = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/pca_test/SDSS_quasar.txt.zip")
>>> data_pca = H2OPrincipalComponentAnalysisEstimator(k=3,
...                                                   score_each_iteration=True,
...                                                   seed=1234,
...                                                   impute_missing=True)
>>> data_pca.train(x=data.names, training_frame=data)
>>> data_pca.show()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.pca.H2OPrincipalComponentAnalysisEstimator.pca_method
documentation:
Specify the algorithm to use for computing the principal components: GramSVD - uses a distributed computation of
the Gram matrix, followed by a local SVD; Power - computes the SVD using the power iteration method
(experimental); Randomized - uses randomized subspace iteration method; GLRM - fits a generalized low-rank model
with L2 loss function and no regularization and solves for the SVD using local matrix algebra (experimental)

Type: `Literal["gram_s_v_d", "power", "randomized", "glrm"]`, defaults to `"gram_s_v_d"`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> data = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/pca_test/SDSS_quasar.txt.zip")
>>> data_pca = H2OPrincipalComponentAnalysisEstimator(k=-1,
...                                                   transform="standardize",
...                                                   pca_method="power",
...                                                   impute_missing=True,
...                                                   max_iterations=800)
>>> data_pca.train(x=data.names, training_frame=data)
>>> data_pca.show()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.pca.H2OPrincipalComponentAnalysisEstimator.pca_impl
documentation:
Specify the implementation to use for computing PCA (via SVD or EVD): MTJ_EVD_DENSEMATRIX - eigenvalue
decompositions for dense matrix using MTJ; MTJ_EVD_SYMMMATRIX - eigenvalue decompositions for symmetric matrix
using MTJ; MTJ_SVD_DENSEMATRIX - singular-value decompositions for dense matrix using MTJ; JAMA - eigenvalue
decompositions for dense matrix using JAMA. References: JAMA - <http://math.nist.gov/javanumerics/jama/>; MTJ -
<https://github.com/fommil/matrix-toolkits-java/>

Type: `Literal["mtj_evd_densematrix", "mtj_evd_symmmatrix", "mtj_svd_densematrix", "jama"]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> data = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/pca_test/SDSS_quasar.txt.zip")
>>> data_pca = H2OPrincipalComponentAnalysisEstimator(k=3,
...                                                   pca_impl="jama",
...                                                   impute_missing=True,
...                                                   max_iterations=1200)
>>> data_pca.train(x=data.names, training_frame=data)
>>> data_pca.show()
```
