methods.name: h2o.estimators.deeplearning.H2ODeepLearningEstimator.classification_stop
documentation:
Stopping criterion for classification error fraction on training data (-1 to disable).

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> covtype = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/covtype/covtype.20k.data")
>>> covtype[54] = covtype[54].asfactor()
>>> predictors = covtype.columns[0:54]
>>> response = 'C55'
>>> train, valid = covtype.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> cars_dl = H2ODeepLearningEstimator(classification_stop=1.5,
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> cov_dl.train(x=predictors,
...              y=response,
...              training_frame=train,
...              validation_frame=valid)
>>> cov_dl.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.deeplearning.H2ODeepLearningEstimator.regression_stop
documentation:
Stopping criterion for regression error (MSE) on training data (-1 to disable).

Type: `float`, defaults to `1e-06`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"]= airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"]= airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> airlines_dl = H2ODeepLearningEstimator(regression_stop=1e-6,
...                                        seed=1234)
>>> airlines_dl.train(x=predictors,
...                   y=response,
...                   training_frame=train,
...                   validation_frame=valid)
>>> airlines_dl.auc()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.deeplearning.H2ODeepLearningEstimator.stopping_rounds
documentation:
Early stopping based on convergence of stopping_metric. Stop if simple moving average of length k of the
stopping_metric does not improve for k:=stopping_rounds scoring events (0 to disable)

Type: `int`, defaults to `5`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"]= airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"]= airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> airlines_dl = H2ODeepLearningEstimator(stopping_metric="auc",
...                                        stopping_rounds=3,
...                                        stopping_tolerance=1e-2,
...                                        seed=1234)
>>> airlines_dl.train(x=predictors,
...                   y=response,
...                   training_frame=train,
...                   validation_frame=valid)
>>> airlines_dl.auc()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.deeplearning.H2ODeepLearningEstimator.stopping_metric
documentation:
Metric to use for early stopping (AUTO: logloss for classification, deviance for regression and anomaly_score
for Isolation Forest). Note that custom and custom_increasing can only be used in GBM and DRF with the Python
client.

Type: `Literal["auto", "deviance", "logloss", "mse", "rmse", "mae", "rmsle", "auc", "aucpr", "lift_top_group",
"misclassification", "mean_per_class_error", "custom", "custom_increasing"]`, defaults to `"auto"`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"]= airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"]= airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> airlines_dl = H2ODeepLearningEstimator(stopping_metric="auc",
...                                        stopping_rounds=3,
...                                        stopping_tolerance=1e-2,
...                                        seed=1234)
>>> airlines_dl.train(x=predictors,
...                   y=response,
...                   training_frame=train,
...                   validation_frame=valid)
>>> airlines_dl.auc()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.deeplearning.H2ODeepLearningEstimator.stopping_tolerance
documentation:
Relative tolerance for metric-based stopping criterion (stop if relative improvement is not at least this much)

```

```

```pycon

>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",

>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> airlines_dl = H2ODeepLearningEstimator(stopping_metric="auc",
...                                        stopping_rounds=3,
...                                        stopping_tolerance=1e-2,
...                                        seed=1234)
>>> airlines_dl.train(x=predictors,
...                   y=response,
...                   training_frame=train,
...                   validation_frame=valid)
>>> airlines_dl.auc()
```

```
examples
```


methods.name: h2o.estimators.deeplearning.H2ODeepLearningEstimator.missing_values_handling

Handling of missing values. Either MeanImputation or Skip.

```pycon
>>> boston['chas'] = boston['chas'].asfactor()

>>> train, valid = boston.split_frame(ratios=[.8])

>>> boston_dl.train(x=predictors,
...                 y=response,
...                 training_frame=train,
...                 validation_frame=valid)
>>> boston_dl.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.deeplearning.H2ODeepLearningEstimator.average_activation

Average activation for sparse auto-encoder. #Experimental

```
examples
```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")

...               training_frame=train,
...               validation_frame=valid)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.deeplearning.H2ODeepLearningEstimator.sparsity_beta
documentation:
Sparsity regularization. #Experimental

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> from h2o.estimators import H2OAutoEncoderEstimator
>>> resp = 784
>>> nfeatures = 20
>>> train = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/bigdata/laptop/mnist/train.csv.gz")
>>> test = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/bigdata/laptop/mnist/test.csv.gz")
>>> train[resp] = train[resp].asfactor()
>>> test[resp] = test[resp].asfactor()
>>> sid = train[0].runif(0)
>>> train_unsupervised = train[sid>=0.5]
>>> train_unsupervised.pop(resp)
>>> ae_model = H2OAutoEncoderEstimator(activation="Tanh",
...                                    hidden=[nfeatures],
...                                    epochs=1,
...                                    ignore_const_cols=False,
...                                    reproducible=True,
...                                    sparsity_beta=0.5,
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> ae_model.train(list(range(resp)),
...                training_frame=train_unsupervised)
>>> ae_model.mse()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.deeplearning.H2ODeepLearningEstimator.max_categorical_features
documentation:
Max. number of categorical features, enforced via hashing. #Experimental

```
examples
```

```pycon
>>> covtype = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/covtype/covtype.20k.data")
>>> covtype[54] = covtype[54].asfactor()
>>> predictors = covtype.columns[0:54]
>>> response = 'C55'
>>> train, valid = covtype.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)

>>> cov_dl.train(x=predictors,
...              y=response,
...              training_frame=train,

>>> cov_dl.logloss()
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.deeplearning.H2ODeepLearningEstimator.reproducible

documentation:
Gains/Lift table number of bins. 0 means disabled.. Default value -1 means automatic binning.

Deep Learning

Build a Deep Neural Network model using CPUs
Builds a feed-forward multilayer artificial neural network on an H2OFrame

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> from h2o.estimators.deeplearning import H2ODeepLearningEstimator
>>> rows = [[1,2,3,4,0], [2,1,2,4,1], [2,1,4,2,1],
...         [0,1,2,34,1], [2,3,4,1,0]] * 50
>>> fr = h2o.H2OFrame(rows)
>>> fr[4] = fr[4].asfactor()
>>> model = H2ODeepLearningEstimator()
>>> model.train(x=range(4), y=4, training_frame=fr)
>>> model.logloss()
```

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators import H2OUpliftRandomForestEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> data = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/uplift/criteo_uplift_13k.csv")
>>> predictors = ["f1", "f2", "f3", "f4", "f5", "f6","f7", "f8"]
>>> response = "conversion"
>>> data[response] = data[response].asfactor()
>>> treatment_column = "treatment"
>>> data[treatment_column] = data[treatment_column].asfactor()
>>> train, valid = data.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> uplift_model = H2OUpliftRandomForestEstimator(ntrees=10,
...                                               max_depth=5,
...                                               uplift_metric="KL",
...                                               min_rows=10,
...                                               seed=1234,
...                                               auuc_type="qini",
...                                               treatment_column=treatment_column)
>>> uplift_model.train(x=predictors,
...                    y=response,
...                    training_frame=train,
...                    validation_frame=valid)
>>> uplift_model.model_performance()
```

classes.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.__init__
documentation:
---
'param ': |-
  Regularization strength
  Defaults to `[0.0]`.
param alpha: |-
  Distribution of regularization between the L1 (Lasso) and L2 (Ridge) penalties. A value of 1 for
  alpha represents Lasso regression, a value of 0 produces Ridge regression, and anything in between
  specifies the amount of mixing between the two. Default value of alpha is 0 when SOLVER = 'L-BFGS'; 0.5
  otherwise.
  Defaults to `None`.
param balance_classes: |-
  Balance training data class counts via over/under-sampling (for imbalanced data).
  Defaults to `False`.
param beta_constraints: |-
  Beta constraints
  Defaults to `None`.
param beta_epsilon: |-
  Converge if beta changes less (using L-infinity norm) than beta esilon, ONLY applies to
  IRLSM solver
  Defaults to `0.0001`.
param build_glm_model: |-
  For maxrsweep mode only. If true, will return full blown GLM models with the desired
  predictorsubsets. If false, only the predictor subsets, predictor coefficients are returned. This is
  forspeeding up the model selection process. The users can choose to build the GLM models themselvesby
  using the predictor subsets themselves. Defaults to false.
  Defaults to `False`.
param class_sampling_factors: |-
  Desired over/under-sampling ratios per class (in lexicographic order). If not
  specified, sampling factors will be automatically computed to obtain class balance during training.
  Requires balance_classes.
  Defaults to `None`.
param cold_start: |-
  Only applicable to multiple alpha/lambda values. If false, build the next model for next set
  of alpha/lambda values starting from the values provided by current model. If true will start GLM model
  from scratch.
  Defaults to `False`.
param compute_p_values: |-
  Request p-values computation, p-values work only with IRLSM solver and no
  regularization
  Defaults to `False`.
param early_stopping: |-
  Stop early when there is no more relative improvement on train or validation (if
  provided)
  Defaults to `False`.
param family: |-
  Family. For maxr/maxrsweep, only gaussian. For backward, ordinal and multinomial families are
  not supported
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param fold_assignment: |-
  Cross-validation fold assignment scheme, if fold_column is not specified. The
  'Stratified' option will stratify the folds based on the response variable, for classification problems.
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param fold_column: |-
  Column with cross-validation fold index assignment per observation.
  Defaults to `None`.
param gradient_epsilon: |-
  Converge if objective changes less (using L-infinity norm) than this, ONLY applies to
  L-BFGS solver. Default (of -1.0) indicates: If lambda_search is set to False and lambda is equal to zero,
  the default value of gradient_epsilon is equal to .000001, otherwise the default value is .0001. If
  lambda_search is set to True, the conditional values above are 1E-8 and 1E-6 respectively.
  Defaults to `-1.0`.
param ignore_const_cols: |-
  Ignore constant columns.
  Defaults to `True`.
param ignored_columns: |-
  Names of columns to ignore for training.
  Defaults to `None`.
param influence: |-
  If set to dfbetas will calculate the difference in beta when a datarow is included and
  excluded in the dataset.
  Defaults to `None`.
param intercept: |-
  Include constant term in the model
  Defaults to `True`.
param lambda_min_ratio: |-
  Minimum lambda used in lambda search, specified as a ratio of lambda_max (the smallest
  lambda that drives all coefficients to zero). Default indicates: if the number of observations is greater
  than the number of variables, then lambda_min_ratio is set to 0.0001; if the number of observations is
  less than the number of variables, then lambda_min_ratio is set to 0.01.
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param lambda_search: |-
  Use lambda search starting at lambda max, given lambda is then interpreted as lambda min
  Defaults to `False`.
param link: |-
  Link function.
  Defaults to `"family_default"`.
param max_active_predictors: |-
  Maximum number of active predictors during computation. Use as a stopping
  criterion to prevent expensive model building with many predictors. Default indicates: If the IRLSM
  solver is used, the value of max_active_predictors is set to 5000 otherwise it is set to 100000000.
  Defaults to `-1`.
param max_after_balance_size: |-
  Maximum relative size of the training data after balancing class counts (can be
  less than 1.0). Requires balance_classes.
  Defaults to `5.0`.
param max_confusion_matrix_size: |-
  [Deprecated] Maximum size (# classes) for confusion matrices to be printed in
  the Logs
  Defaults to `20`.
param max_iterations: |-
  Maximum number of iterations
  Defaults to `0`.
param max_predictor_number: |-
  Maximum number of predictors to be considered when building GLM models. Defaults
  to 1.
  Defaults to `1`.
param max_runtime_secs: |-
  Maximum allowed runtime in seconds for model training. Use 0 to disable.
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param min_predictor_number: |-
  For mode = 'backward' only. Minimum number of predictors to be considered when
  building GLM models starting with all predictors to be included. Defaults to 1.
  Defaults to `1`.
param missing_values_handling: |-
  Handling of missing values. Either MeanImputation, Skip or PlugValues.
  Defaults to `"mean_imputation"`.
param mode: |-
  Mode: Used to choose model selection algorithms to use. Options include 'allsubsets' for all
  subsets, 'maxr' that uses sequential replacement and GLM to build all models, slow but works with cross-
  validation, validation frames for more robust results, 'maxrsweep' that uses sequential replacement and
  sweeping action, much faster than 'maxr', 'backward' for backward selection.
  Defaults to `"maxr"`.
param model_id: |-
  Destination id for this model; auto-generated if not specified.
  Defaults to `None`.
param multinode_mode: |-
  For maxrsweep only. If enabled, will attempt to perform sweeping action using multiple
  nodes in the cluster. Defaults to false.
  Defaults to `False`.
param nfolds: |-
  Number of folds for K-fold cross-validation (0 to disable or >= 2).
  Defaults to `0`.
param nlambdas: |-
  Number of lambdas to be used in a search. Default indicates: If alpha is zero, with lambda
  search set to True, the value of nlamdas is set to 30 (fewer lambdas are needed for ridge regression)
  otherwise it is set to 100.
  Defaults to `0`.
param non_negative: |-
  Restrict coefficients (not intercept) to be non-negative
  Defaults to `False`.
param nparallelism: |-
  number of models to build in parallel. Defaults to 0.0 which is adaptive to the system
  capability
  Defaults to `0`.
param obj_reg: |-
  Likelihood divider in objective value computation, default (of -1.0) will set it to 1/nobs
  Defaults to `-1.0`.
param objective_epsilon: |-
  Converge if objective value changes less than this. Default (of -1.0) indicates: If
  lambda_search is set to True the value of objective_epsilon is set to .0001. If the lambda_search is set
  to False and lambda is equal to zero, the value of objective_epsilon is set to .000001, for any other
  value of lambda the default value of objective_epsilon is set to .0001.
  Defaults to `-1.0`.
param offset_column: |-
  Offset column. This will be added to the combination of columns before applying the link
  function.
  Defaults to `None`.
param p_values_threshold: |-
  For mode='backward' only. If specified, will stop the model building process when
  all coefficientsp-values drop below this threshold
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param plug_values: |-
  Plug Values (a single row frame containing values that will be used to impute missing values
  of the training/validation frame, use with conjunction missing_values_handling = PlugValues)
  Defaults to `None`.
param prior: |-
  Prior probability for y==1. To be used only for logistic regression iff the data has been sampled
  and the mean of response does not reflect reality.
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param remove_collinear_columns: |-
  In case of linearly dependent columns, remove some of the dependent columns
  Defaults to `False`.
param response_column: |-
  Response variable column.
  Defaults to `None`.
param score_each_iteration: |-
  Whether to score during each iteration of model training.
  Defaults to `False`.
param score_iteration_interval: |-
  Perform scoring for every score_iteration_interval iterations
  Defaults to `0`.
param seed: |-
  Seed for pseudo random number generator (if applicable)
  Defaults to `-1`.
param solver: |-
  AUTO will set the solver based on given data and the other parameters. IRLSM is fast on on
  problems with small number of predictors and for lambda-search with L1 penalty, L_BFGS scales better for
  datasets with many columns.
  Defaults to `"irlsm"`.
param standardize: |-
  Standardize numeric columns to have zero mean and unit variance
  Defaults to `True`.
param startval: |-
  Double array to initialize coefficients for GLM.
  Defaults to `None`.
param stopping_metric: |-
  Metric to use for early stopping (AUTO: logloss for classification, deviance for
  regression and anomaly_score for Isolation Forest). Note that custom and custom_increasing can only be
  used in GBM and DRF with the Python client.
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param stopping_rounds: |-
  Early stopping based on convergence of stopping_metric. Stop if simple moving average of
  length k of the stopping_metric does not improve for k:=stopping_rounds scoring events (0 to disable)
  Defaults to `0`.
param stopping_tolerance: |-
  Relative tolerance for metric-based stopping criterion (stop if relative improvement
  is not at least this much)
  Defaults to `0.001`.
param theta: |-
  Theta
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param training_frame: |-
  Id of the training data frame.
  Defaults to `None`.
param tweedie_link_power: |-
  Tweedie link power
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param tweedie_variance_power: |-
  Tweedie variance power
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param validation_frame: |-
  Id of the validation data frame.
  Defaults to `None`.
param weights_column: |-
  Column with observation weights. Giving some observation a weight of zero is equivalent
  to excluding it from the dataset; giving an observation a relative weight of 2 is equivalent to repeating
  that row twice. Negative weights are not allowed. Note: Weights are per-row observation weights and do
  not increase the size of the data frame. This is typically the number of times a row is repeated, but
  non-integer values are supported as well. During training, rows with higher weights matter more, due to
  the larger loss function pre-factor. If you set weight = 0 for a row, the returned prediction frame at
  that row is zero and this is incorrect. To get an accurate prediction, remove all rows with weight == 0.
  Defaults to `None`.
'type ': List[float]
type alpha: List[float], optional
type balance_classes: bool
type beta_constraints: Union[None, str, H2OFrame], optional
type beta_epsilon: float
type build_glm_model: bool
type class_sampling_factors: List[float], optional
type cold_start: bool
type compute_p_values: bool
type early_stopping: bool
type family: |-
  Literal["auto", "gaussian", "binomial", "fractionalbinomial", "quasibinomial", "poisson", "gamma",

type fold_assignment: Literal["auto", "random", "modulo", "stratified"]
type fold_column: str, optional
type gradient_epsilon: float
type ignore_const_cols: bool
type ignored_columns: List[str], optional
type influence: Literal["dfbetas"], optional
type intercept: bool
type lambda_min_ratio: float
type lambda_search: bool
type link: Literal["family_default", "identity", "logit", "log", "inverse", "tweedie",
  "ologit"]
type max_active_predictors: int

type mode: Literal["allsubsets", "maxr", "maxrsweep", "backward"]

type standardize: bool

  "misclassification", "mean_per_class_error", "custom", "custom_increasing"]

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.training_frame
documentation:
Id of the training data frame.

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.validation_frame
documentation:
Id of the validation data frame.

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.nfolds
documentation:
Number of folds for K-fold cross-validation (0 to disable or >= 2).

Type: `int`, defaults to `0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.seed
documentation:
Seed for pseudo random number generator (if applicable)

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.fold_assignment
documentation:
Cross-validation fold assignment scheme, if fold_column is not specified. The 'Stratified' option will stratify
the folds based on the response variable, for classification problems.

Type: `Literal["auto", "random", "modulo", "stratified"]`, defaults to `"auto"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.fold_column
documentation:
Column with cross-validation fold index assignment per observation.

Type: `str`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.response_column
documentation:
Response variable column.

Type: `str`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.ignored_columns
documentation:
Names of columns to ignore for training.

Type: `List[str]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.ignore_const_cols
documentation:
Ignore constant columns.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `True`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.score_each_iteration
documentation:
Whether to score during each iteration of model training.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.score_iteration_interval
documentation:
Perform scoring for every score_iteration_interval iterations

Type: `int`, defaults to `0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.offset_column
documentation:
Offset column. This will be added to the combination of columns before applying the link function.

Type: `str`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.weights_column
documentation:
Column with observation weights. Giving some observation a weight of zero is equivalent to excluding it from the
dataset; giving an observation a relative weight of 2 is equivalent to repeating that row twice. Negative
weights are not allowed. Note: Weights are per-row observation weights and do not increase the size of the data
frame. This is typically the number of times a row is repeated, but non-integer values are supported as well.
During training, rows with higher weights matter more, due to the larger loss function pre-factor. If you set
weight = 0 for a row, the returned prediction frame at that row is zero and this is incorrect. To get an
accurate prediction, remove all rows with weight == 0.

Type: `str`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.family
documentation:
Family. For maxr/maxrsweep, only gaussian. For backward, ordinal and multinomial families are not supported

Type: `Literal["auto", "gaussian", "binomial", "fractionalbinomial", "quasibinomial", "poisson", "gamma",
"tweedie", "negativebinomial"]`, defaults to `"auto"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.link
documentation:
Link function.

Type: `Literal["family_default", "identity", "logit", "log", "inverse", "tweedie", "ologit"]`, defaults to
`"family_default"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.tweedie_variance_power
documentation:
Tweedie variance power

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.tweedie_link_power
documentation:
Tweedie link power

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.theta
documentation:
Theta

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.solver
documentation:
AUTO will set the solver based on given data and the other parameters. IRLSM is fast on on problems with small
number of predictors and for lambda-search with L1 penalty, L_BFGS scales better for datasets with many columns.

Type: `Literal["auto", "irlsm", "l_bfgs", "coordinate_descent_naive", "coordinate_descent",
"gradient_descent_lh", "gradient_descent_sqerr"]`, defaults to `"irlsm"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.alpha
documentation:
Distribution of regularization between the L1 (Lasso) and L2 (Ridge) penalties. A value of 1 for alpha
represents Lasso regression, a value of 0 produces Ridge regression, and anything in between specifies the
amount of mixing between the two. Default value of alpha is 0 when SOLVER = 'L-BFGS'; 0.5 otherwise.

Type: `List[float]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.lambda_
documentation:
Regularization strength

Type: `List[float]`, defaults to `[0.0]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.lambda_search
documentation:
Use lambda search starting at lambda max, given lambda is then interpreted as lambda min

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.early_stopping
documentation:
Stop early when there is no more relative improvement on train or validation (if provided)

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.nlambdas
documentation:
Number of lambdas to be used in a search. Default indicates: If alpha is zero, with lambda search set to True,
the value of nlamdas is set to 30 (fewer lambdas are needed for ridge regression) otherwise it is set to 100.

Type: `int`, defaults to `0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.standardize
documentation:
Standardize numeric columns to have zero mean and unit variance

Type: `bool`, defaults to `True`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.missing_values_handling
documentation:
Handling of missing values. Either MeanImputation, Skip or PlugValues.

Type: `Literal["mean_imputation", "skip", "plug_values"]`, defaults to `"mean_imputation"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.plug_values
documentation:
Plug Values (a single row frame containing values that will be used to impute missing values of the
training/validation frame, use with conjunction missing_values_handling = PlugValues)

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.compute_p_values
documentation:
Request p-values computation, p-values work only with IRLSM solver and no regularization

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.remove_collinear_columns
documentation:
In case of linearly dependent columns, remove some of the dependent columns

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.intercept
documentation:
Include constant term in the model

Type: `bool`, defaults to `True`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.non_negative
documentation:
Restrict coefficients (not intercept) to be non-negative

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.max_iterations
documentation:
Maximum number of iterations

Type: `int`, defaults to `0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.objective_epsilon
documentation:
Converge if objective value changes less than this. Default (of -1.0) indicates: If lambda_search is set to
True the value of objective_epsilon is set to .0001. If the lambda_search is set to False and lambda is equal to
zero, the value of objective_epsilon is set to .000001, for any other value of lambda the default value of
objective_epsilon is set to .0001.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.beta_epsilon
documentation:
Converge if beta changes less (using L-infinity norm) than beta esilon, ONLY applies to IRLSM solver

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0001`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.gradient_epsilon
documentation:
Converge if objective changes less (using L-infinity norm) than this, ONLY applies to L-BFGS solver. Default
(of -1.0) indicates: If lambda_search is set to False and lambda is equal to zero, the default value of
gradient_epsilon is equal to .000001, otherwise the default value is .0001. If lambda_search is set to True, the
conditional values above are 1E-8 and 1E-6 respectively.

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.startval
documentation:
Double array to initialize coefficients for GLM.

Type: `List[float]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.prior
documentation:
Prior probability for y==1. To be used only for logistic regression iff the data has been sampled and the mean
of response does not reflect reality.

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.cold_start
documentation:
Only applicable to multiple alpha/lambda values. If false, build the next model for next set of alpha/lambda
values starting from the values provided by current model. If true will start GLM model from scratch.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.lambda_min_ratio
documentation:
Minimum lambda used in lambda search, specified as a ratio of lambda_max (the smallest lambda that drives all
coefficients to zero). Default indicates: if the number of observations is greater than the number of variables,
then lambda_min_ratio is set to 0.0001; if the number of observations is less than the number of variables, then
lambda_min_ratio is set to 0.01.

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.beta_constraints
documentation:
Beta constraints

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.max_active_predictors
documentation:
Maximum number of active predictors during computation. Use as a stopping criterion to prevent expensive model
building with many predictors. Default indicates: If the IRLSM solver is used, the value of
max_active_predictors is set to 5000 otherwise it is set to 100000000.

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.obj_reg
documentation:
Likelihood divider in objective value computation, default (of -1.0) will set it to 1/nobs

Type: `float`, defaults to `-1.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.stopping_rounds
documentation:
Early stopping based on convergence of stopping_metric. Stop if simple moving average of length k of the
stopping_metric does not improve for k:=stopping_rounds scoring events (0 to disable)

Type: `int`, defaults to `0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.stopping_metric
documentation:
Metric to use for early stopping (AUTO: logloss for classification, deviance for regression and anomaly_score
for Isolation Forest). Note that custom and custom_increasing can only be used in GBM and DRF with the Python
client.

Type: `Literal["auto", "deviance", "logloss", "mse", "rmse", "mae", "rmsle", "auc", "aucpr", "lift_top_group",
"misclassification", "mean_per_class_error", "custom", "custom_increasing"]`, defaults to `"auto"`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.stopping_tolerance
documentation:
Relative tolerance for metric-based stopping criterion (stop if relative improvement is not at least this much)

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.001`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.balance_classes
documentation:
Balance training data class counts via over/under-sampling (for imbalanced data).

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.class_sampling_factors
documentation:
Desired over/under-sampling ratios per class (in lexicographic order). If not specified, sampling factors will
be automatically computed to obtain class balance during training. Requires balance_classes.

Type: `List[float]`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.max_after_balance_size
documentation:
Maximum relative size of the training data after balancing class counts (can be less than 1.0). Requires
balance_classes.

Type: `float`, defaults to `5.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.max_confusion_matrix_size
documentation:
[Deprecated] Maximum size (# classes) for confusion matrices to be printed in the Logs

Type: `int`, defaults to `20`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.max_runtime_secs
documentation:
Maximum allowed runtime in seconds for model training. Use 0 to disable.

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.nparallelism
documentation:
number of models to build in parallel. Defaults to 0.0 which is adaptive to the system capability

Type: `int`, defaults to `0`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.max_predictor_number
documentation:
Maximum number of predictors to be considered when building GLM models. Defaults to 1.

Type: `int`, defaults to `1`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.min_predictor_number
documentation:
For mode = 'backward' only. Minimum number of predictors to be considered when building GLM models starting
with all predictors to be included. Defaults to 1.

Type: `int`, defaults to `1`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.mode
documentation:
Mode: Used to choose model selection algorithms to use. Options include 'allsubsets' for all subsets, 'maxr'
that uses sequential replacement and GLM to build all models, slow but works with cross-validation, validation
frames for more robust results, 'maxrsweep' that uses sequential replacement and sweeping action, much faster
than 'maxr', 'backward' for backward selection.

Type: `Literal["allsubsets", "maxr", "maxrsweep", "backward"]`, defaults to `"maxr"`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators import H2OModelSelectionEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> prostate = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/logreg/prostate.csv")
>>> predictors = ["AGE", "RACE", "CAPSULE", "DCAPS", "PSA", "VOL", "DPROS"]
>>> response = "GLEASON"
>>> maxrModel = H2OModelSelectionEstimator(max_predictor_number=5,
...                                        seed=12345,
...                                        mode="maxr")
>>> maxrModel.train(x=predictors, y=response, training_frame=prostate)
>>> results = maxrModel.result()
>>> print(results)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.build_glm_model
documentation:
For maxrsweep mode only. If true, will return full blown GLM models with the desired predictorsubsets. If
false, only the predictor subsets, predictor coefficients are returned. This is forspeeding up the model
selection process. The users can choose to build the GLM models themselvesby using the predictor subsets
themselves. Defaults to false.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators import H2OModelSelectionEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> prostate = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/logreg/prostate.csv")
>>> predictors = ["AGE", "RACE", "CAPSULE", "DCAPS", "PSA", "VOL", "DPROS"]
>>> response = "GLEASON"
>>> maxrModel = H2OModelSelectionEstimator(max_predictor_number=5,
...                                        seed=12345,
...                                        mode="maxrsweep",
...                                        build_glm_model=True)
>>> maxrModel.train(x=predictors, y=response, training_frame=prostate)
>>> result = maxrModel.result()
>>> # get the GLM model with the best performance for a fixed predictor size:
>>> one_model = h2o.get_model(result["model_id"][1, 0])
>>> predict = one_model.predict(prostate)
>>> # print a version of the predict frame:
>>> print(predict)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.p_values_threshold
documentation:
For mode='backward' only. If specified, will stop the model building process when all coefficientsp-values drop
below this threshold

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators import H2OModelSelectionEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> prostate = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/logreg/prostate.csv")
>>> predictors = ["AGE", "RACE", "CAPSULE", DCAPS", "PSA", "VOL", "DPROS"]
>>> response = "GLEASON"
>>> backwardModel = H2OModelSelectionEstimator(min_predictor_number=2,
...                                            seed=12345,
...                                            mode="backward",
...                                            p_values_threshold=0.001)
>>> backwardModel.train(x=predictors, y=response, training_frame=prostate)
>>> result = backwardModel.result()
>>> print(result)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.influence
documentation:
If set to dfbetas will calculate the difference in beta when a datarow is included and excluded in the dataset.

Type: `Literal["dfbetas"]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators import H2OModelSelectionEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> prostate = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/logreg/prostate.csv")
>>> predictors = ["AGE", "RACE", "CAPSULE", "DCAPS", "PSA", "VOL", "DPROS"]
>>> response = "GLEASON"
>>> maxrModel = H2OModelSelectionEstimator(max_predictor_number=5,
...                                        seed=12345,
...                                        mode="maxr",
...                                        influence="dfbetas")
>>> maxrModel.train(x=predictors, y=response, training_frame=prostate)
>>> glm_rid = maxrModel.get_regression_influence_diagnostics()
>>> print(glm_rid)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.multinode_mode
documentation:
For maxrsweep only. If enabled, will attempt to perform sweeping action using multiple nodes in the cluster.
Defaults to false.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.get_regression_influence_diagnostics
documentation:
Get the regression influence diagnostics frames for all models with different number of predictors. If a
predictor size is specified, only one frame is returned for that predictor size.

```
param predictor_size

predictor subset size, will return regression influence diagnostics frame of that size

return

list of H2OFrames or just one frame that contains predictors, response and DFBETA predictors
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.coef_norm
documentation:
Get the normalized coefficients for all models built with different number of predictors.

```
param predictor_size

predictor subset size, will only return model coefficients of that subset size.

return

list of Python Dicts of coefficients for all models built with different predictor numbers

examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators import H2OModelSelectionEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> prostate = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/logreg/prostate.csv")
>>> predictors = ["AGE", "RACE", "CAPSULE", "DCAPS", "PSA", "VOL", "DPROS"]
>>> response = "GLEASON"
>>> maxrModel = H2OModelSelectionEstimator(max_predictor_number=5,
...                                        seed=12345,
...                                        mode="maxr")
>>> maxrModel.train(x=predictors, y=response, training_frame=prostate)
>>> coeff_norm = maxrModel.coef_norm()
>>> print(coeff_norm)
>>> coeff_norm_3 = maxrModel.coef_norm(predictor_size=3) # print coefficient norm with 3 predictors
>>> print(coeff_norm_3)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.model_selection.H2OModelSelectionEstimator.coef
documentation:
Get the coefficients for all models built with different number of predictors.

```
param predictor_size

predictor subset size, will only return model coefficients of that subset size.

return

list of Python Dicts of coefficients for all models built with different predictor numbers

examples

```

```pycon
>>> import h2o
>>> from h2o.estimators import H2OModelSelectionEstimator
>>> h2o.init()
>>> prostate = h2o.import_file("http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/logreg/prostate.csv")
>>> predictors = ["AGE", "RACE", "CAPSULE", "DCAPS", "PSA", "VOL", "DPROS"]
>>> response = "GLEASON"
>>> maxrModel = H2OModelSelectionEstimator(max_predictor_number=5,
...                                        seed=12345,
...                                        mode="maxr")
>>> maxrModel.train(x=predictors, y=response, training_frame=prostate)
>>> coeff = maxrModel.coef()
>>> print(coeff)
>>> coeff_3 = maxrModel.coef(predictor_size=3)
>>> print(coeff_3)
```

Get list of best models with 1 predictor, 2 predictors, ..., max_predictor_number of predictors that have the
highest r2 values

```

a list of best predictors subset
```

Model Selection

H2O ModelSelection is used to build the best model with one predictor, two predictors, ... up to max_predictor_number 
specified in the algorithm parameters when mode=allsubsets.  The best model is the one with the highest R2 value.  When
mode=maxr, the model returned is no longer guaranteed to have the best R2 value.

modules.name: h2o.job
documentation:
Handler to an asynchronous task executed on the remote server.

A job is an object with states: CREATED, RUNNING, DONE, FAILED, CANCELLED
A job can be polled for completion and reports the current progress as long as it is RUNNING.
-*- encoding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: h2o.job._DefaultJobProgressWidgetFactory
documentation: Factory descriptor creating the job progress rendering widgets: this method is called by default for every running job.

classes.name: h2o.job.H2OJob

methods.name: h2o.job.H2OJob.__init__
documentation: Initialize new H2OJob object.

methods.name: h2o.job.H2OJob.poll
documentation:
Wait until the job finishes.

This method will continuously query the server about the status of the job, until the job reaches a
completion. During this time we will display (in stdout) a progress bar with % completion status.
\:param poll_updates: a callback function called a each polling iteration with 2 arguments:

> (current_job: H2OJob, bar_progression: float)

methods.name: h2o.job.H2OJob.poll_once
documentation: Query the job status and show the progress bar, but then cancel immediately.

param auc_type: |-
  Set default multinomial AUC type.
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param balance_classes: |-
  Balance training data class counts via over/under-sampling (for imbalanced data).
  Defaults to `False`.
param binomial_double_trees: |-
  For binary classification: Build 2x as many trees (one per class) - can lead to
  higher accuracy.
  Defaults to `False`.
param build_tree_one_node: |-
  Run on one node only; no network overhead but fewer cpus used. Suitable for small
  datasets.
  Defaults to `False`.
param calibrate_model: |-
  Use Platt Scaling (default) or Isotonic Regression to calculate calibrated class
  probabilities. Calibration can provide more accurate estimates of class probabilities.
  Defaults to `False`.
param calibration_frame: |-
  Data for model calibration
  Defaults to `None`.
param calibration_method: |-
  Calibration method to use
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param categorical_encoding: |-
  Encoding scheme for categorical features
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param check_constant_response: |-
  Check if response column is constant. If enabled, then an exception is thrown if
  the response column is a constant value.If disabled, then model will train regardless of the response
  column being a constant value or not.
  Defaults to `True`.
param checkpoint: |-
  Model checkpoint to resume training with.
  Defaults to `None`.
param class_sampling_factors: |-
  Desired over/under-sampling ratios per class (in lexicographic order). If not
  specified, sampling factors will be automatically computed to obtain class balance during training.
  Requires balance_classes.
  Defaults to `None`.
param col_sample_rate_change_per_level: |-
  Relative change of the column sampling rate for every level (must be >
  0.0 and \<= 2.0)
  Defaults to `1.0`.
param col_sample_rate_per_tree: |-
  Column sample rate per tree (from 0.0 to 1.0)
  Defaults to `1.0`.
param custom_metric_func: |-
  Reference to custom evaluation function, format: `language:keyName=funcName`
  Defaults to `None`.
param distribution: |-
  Distribution function
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param export_checkpoints_dir: |-
  Automatically export generated models to this directory.
  Defaults to `None`.
param fold_assignment: |-
  Cross-validation fold assignment scheme, if fold_column is not specified. The
  'Stratified' option will stratify the folds based on the response variable, for classification problems.
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param fold_column: |-
  Column with cross-validation fold index assignment per observation.
  Defaults to `None`.
param gainslift_bins: |-
  Gains/Lift table number of bins. 0 means disabled.. Default value -1 means automatic
  binning.
  Defaults to `-1`.
param histogram_type: |-
  What type of histogram to use for finding optimal split points
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param ignore_const_cols: |-
  Ignore constant columns.
  Defaults to `True`.
param ignored_columns: |-
  Names of columns to ignore for training.
  Defaults to `None`.
param keep_cross_validation_fold_assignment: |-
  Whether to keep the cross-validation fold assignment.
  Defaults to `False`.
param keep_cross_validation_models: |-
  Whether to keep the cross-validation models.
  Defaults to `True`.
param keep_cross_validation_predictions: |-
  Whether to keep the predictions of the cross-validation models.
  Defaults to `False`.
param max_after_balance_size: |-
  Maximum relative size of the training data after balancing class counts (can be
  less than 1.0). Requires balance_classes.
  Defaults to `5.0`.
param max_confusion_matrix_size: |-
  [Deprecated] Maximum size (# classes) for confusion matrices to be printed in
  the Logs
  Defaults to `20`.
param max_depth: |-
  Maximum tree depth (0 for unlimited).
  Defaults to `20`.
param max_runtime_secs: |-
  Maximum allowed runtime in seconds for model training. Use 0 to disable.
  Defaults to `0.0`.
param min_rows: |-
  Fewest allowed (weighted) observations in a leaf.
  Defaults to `1.0`.
param min_split_improvement: |-
  Minimum relative improvement in squared error reduction for a split to happen
  Defaults to `1e-05`.
param model_id: |-
  Destination id for this model; auto-generated if not specified.
  Defaults to `None`.
param mtries: |-
  Number of variables randomly sampled as candidates at each split. If set to -1, defaults to
  sqrt\{p} for classification and p/3 for regression (where p is the # of predictors
  Defaults to `-1`.
param nbins: |-
  For numerical columns (real/int), build a histogram of (at least) this many bins, then split at
  the best point
  Defaults to `20`.
param nbins_cats: |-
  For categorical columns (factors), build a histogram of this many bins, then split at the
  best point. Higher values can lead to more overfitting.
  Defaults to `1024`.
param nbins_top_level: |-
  For numerical columns (real/int), build a histogram of (at most) this many bins at the
  root level, then decrease by factor of two per level
  Defaults to `1024`.
param nfolds: |-
  Number of folds for K-fold cross-validation (0 to disable or >= 2).
  Defaults to `0`.
param ntrees: |-
  Number of trees.
  Defaults to `50`.
param r2_stopping: "r2_stopping is no longer supported and will be ignored if set\
  \ - please use stopping_rounds,\nstopping_metric and stopping_tolerance instead.\
  \ Previous version of H2O would stop making trees when the\nR^2 metric equals or\
  \ exceeds this\nDefaults to `\u221E`."
param response_column: |-

  Defaults to `None`.
param sample_rate: |-

  Defaults to `0.632`.
param sample_rate_per_class: |-

  to 1.0), for each tree
  Defaults to `None`.
param score_each_iteration: |-
  Whether to score during each iteration of model training.
  Defaults to `False`.
param score_tree_interval: |-
  Score the model after every so many trees. Disabled if set to 0.
  Defaults to `0`.
param seed: |-
  Seed for pseudo random number generator (if applicable)
  Defaults to `-1`.
param stopping_metric: |-
  Metric to use for early stopping (AUTO: logloss for classification, deviance for
  regression and anomaly_score for Isolation Forest). Note that custom and custom_increasing can only be
  used in GBM and DRF with the Python client.
  Defaults to `"auto"`.
param stopping_rounds: |-
  Early stopping based on convergence of stopping_metric. Stop if simple moving average of
  length k of the stopping_metric does not improve for k:=stopping_rounds scoring events (0 to disable)
  Defaults to `0`.
param stopping_tolerance: |-
  Relative tolerance for metric-based stopping criterion (stop if relative improvement
  is not at least this much)
  Defaults to `0.001`.
param training_frame: |-
  Id of the training data frame.
  Defaults to `None`.
param validation_frame: |-
  Id of the validation data frame.
  Defaults to `None`.
param weights_column: |-
  Column with observation weights. Giving some observation a weight of zero is equivalent
  to excluding it from the dataset; giving an observation a relative weight of 2 is equivalent to repeating
  that row twice. Negative weights are not allowed. Note: Weights are per-row observation weights and do
  not increase the size of the data frame. This is typically the number of times a row is repeated, but
  non-integer values are supported as well. During training, rows with higher weights matter more, due to
  the larger loss function pre-factor. If you set weight = 0 for a row, the returned prediction frame at
  that row is zero and this is incorrect. To get an accurate prediction, remove all rows with weight == 0.
  Defaults to `None`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.training_frame
documentation:
Id of the training data frame.

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OFrame]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars["economy_20mpg"] = cars["economy_20mpg"].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","acceleration","year"]
>>> response = "economy_20mpg"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8],
...                                 seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_drf.auc(valid=True)
```

Column with observation weights. Giving some observation a weight of zero is equivalent to excluding it from the
dataset; giving an observation a relative weight of 2 is equivalent to repeating that row twice. Negative
weights are not allowed. Note: Weights are per-row observation weights and do not increase the size of the data
frame. This is typically the number of times a row is repeated, but non-integer values are supported as well.
During training, rows with higher weights matter more, due to the larger loss function pre-factor. If you set
weight = 0 for a row, the returned prediction frame at that row is zero and this is incorrect. To get an
accurate prediction, remove all rows with weight == 0.

Type: `str`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars["economy_20mpg"] = cars["economy_20mpg"].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","acceleration","year"]
>>> response = "economy_20mpg"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8],
...                                 seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid,
...                weights_column="weight")
>>> cars_drf.auc(valid=True)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.balance_classes
documentation:
Balance training data class counts via over/under-sampling (for imbalanced data).

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> covtype = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/covtype/covtype.20k.data")
>>> covtype[54] = covtype[54].asfactor()
>>> predictors = covtype.columns[0:54]
>>> response = 'C55'
>>> train, valid = covtype.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> cov_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(balance_classes=True,
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> cov_drf.train(x=predictors,
...               y=response,
...               training_frame=train,
...               validation_frame=valid)
>>> print('logloss', cov_drf.logloss(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.class_sampling_factors
documentation:
Desired over/under-sampling ratios per class (in lexicographic order). If not specified, sampling factors will
be automatically computed to obtain class balance during training. Requires balance_classes.

Type: `List[float]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> covtype = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/covtype/covtype.20k.data")
>>> covtype[54] = covtype[54].asfactor()
>>> predictors = covtype.columns[0:54]
>>> response = 'C55'
>>> train, valid = covtype.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> print(covtype[54].table())
>>> sample_factors = [1., 0.5, 1., 1., 1., 1., 1.]
>>> cov_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(balance_classes=True,
...                                    class_sampling_factors=sample_factors,
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> cov_drf.train(x=predictors,
...               y=response,
...               training_frame=train,
...               validation_frame=valid)
>>> print('logloss', cov_drf.logloss(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.max_after_balance_size
documentation:
Maximum relative size of the training data after balancing class counts (can be less than 1.0). Requires
balance_classes.

Type: `float`, defaults to `5.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> covtype = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/covtype/covtype.20k.data")
>>> covtype[54] = covtype[54].asfactor()
>>> predictors = covtype.columns[0:54]
>>> response = 'C55'
>>> train, valid = covtype.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> print(covtype[54].table())
>>> max = .85
>>> cov_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(balance_classes=True,
...                                    max_after_balance_size=max,
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> cov_drf.train(x=predictors,
...               y=response,
...               training_frame=train,
...               validation_frame=valid)
>>> print('logloss', cov_drf.logloss(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.max_confusion_matrix_size
documentation:
[Deprecated] Maximum size (# classes) for confusion matrices to be printed in the Logs

Type: `int`, defaults to `20`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.ntrees
documentation:
Number of trees.

Type: `int`, defaults to `50`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> titanic = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/gbm_test/titanic.csv")
>>> titanic['survived'] = titanic['survived'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = titanic.columns
>>> del predictors[1:3]
>>> response = 'survived'
>>> train, valid = titanic.split_frame(ratios=[.8],
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> tree_num = [20, 50, 80, 110,
...             140, 170, 200]
>>> label = ["20", "50", "80", "110",
...          "140", "170", "200"]
>>> for key, num in enumerate(tree_num):
#              Input an integer for 'num' and 'key'
>>> titanic_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(ntrees=num,
...                                        seed=1234)
>>> titanic_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                   y=response,
...                   training_frame=train,
...                   validation_frame=valid)
>>> print(label[key], 'training score',
...       titanic_drf.auc(train=True))
>>> print(label[key], 'validation score',
...       titanic_drf.auc(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.max_depth
documentation:
Maximum tree depth (0 for unlimited).

Type: `int`, defaults to `20`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> df = h2o.import_file(path = "http://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/gbm_test/titanic.csv")
>>> response = "survived"
>>> df[response] = df[response].asfactor()
>>> predictors = df.columns
>>> del predictors[1:3]
>>> train, valid, test = df.split_frame(ratios=[0.6,0.2],
...                                     seed=1234,
...                                     destination_frames=
...                                     ['train.hex','valid.hex','test.hex'])
>>> drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator()
>>> drf.train(x=predictors,
...           y=response,
...           training_frame=train)
>>> perf = drf.model_performance(valid)
>>> print(perf.auc())
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.min_rows
documentation:
Fewest allowed (weighted) observations in a leaf.

Type: `float`, defaults to `1.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars["economy_20mpg"] = cars["economy_20mpg"].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","acceleration","year"]
>>> response = "economy_20mpg"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(min_rows=16,
...                                     seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> print(cars_drf.auc(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.nbins
documentation:
For numerical columns (real/int), build a histogram of (at least) this many bins, then split at the best point

Type: `int`, defaults to `20`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> eeg = h2o.import_file("https://h2o-public-test-data.s3.amazonaws.com/smalldata/eeg/eeg_eyestate.csv")
>>> eeg['eyeDetection'] = eeg['eyeDetection'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = eeg.columns[:-1]
>>> response = 'eyeDetection'
>>> train, valid = eeg.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> bin_num = [16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512]
>>> label = ["16", "32", "64", "128", "256", "512"]
>>> for key, num in enumerate(bin_num):
#              Insert integer for 'num' and 'key'
>>> eeg_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(nbins=num, seed=1234)
>>> eeg_drf.train(x=predictors,
...               y=response,
...               training_frame=train,
...               validation_frame=valid)
>>> print(label[key], 'training score',
...       eeg_drf.auc(train=True))
>>> print(label[key], 'validation score',
...       eeg_drf.auc(train=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.nbins_top_level
documentation:
For numerical columns (real/int), build a histogram of (at most) this many bins at the root level, then decrease
by factor of two per level

Type: `int`, defaults to `1024`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> eeg = h2o.import_file("https://h2o-public-test-data.s3.amazonaws.com/smalldata/eeg/eeg_eyestate.csv")
>>> eeg['eyeDetection'] = eeg['eyeDetection'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = eeg.columns[:-1]
>>> response = 'eyeDetection'
>>> train, valid = eeg.split_frame(ratios=[.8],
...                                seed=1234)
>>> bin_num = [32, 64, 128, 256, 512,
...            1024, 2048, 4096]
>>> label = ["32", "64", "128", "256",
...          "512", "1024", "2048", "4096"]
>>> for key, num in enumerate(bin_num):
#              Insert integer for 'num' and 'key'
>>> eeg_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(nbins_top_level=32,
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> eeg_drf.train(x=predictors,
...               y=response,
...               training_frame=train,
...               validation_frame=valid)
>>> print(label[key], 'training score',
...       eeg_gbm.auc(train=True))
>>> print(label[key], 'validation score',
...       eeg_gbm.auc(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.nbins_cats
documentation:
For categorical columns (factors), build a histogram of this many bins, then split at the best point. Higher
values can lead to more overfitting.

Type: `int`, defaults to `1024`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"] = airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"] = airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> bin_num = [8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256,
...            512, 1024, 2048, 4096]
>>> label = ["8", "16", "32", "64", "128",
...          "256", "512", "1024", "2048", "4096"]
>>> for key, num in enumerate(bin_num):
#              Insert integer for 'num' and 'key'
>>> airlines_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(nbins_cats=num,
...                                         seed=1234)
>>> airlines_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                    y=response,
...                    training_frame=train,
...                    validation_frame=valid)
>>> print(label[key], 'training score',
...       airlines_gbm.auc(train=True))
>>> print(label[key], 'validation score',
...       airlines_gbm.auc(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.r2_stopping
documentation:
r2_stopping is no longer supported and will be ignored if set - please use stopping_rounds, stopping_metric and
stopping_tolerance instead. Previous version of H2O would stop making trees when the R^2 metric equals or
exceeds this

Type: `float`, defaults to `∞`.

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.stopping_rounds
documentation:
Early stopping based on convergence of stopping_metric. Stop if simple moving average of length k of the
stopping_metric does not improve for k:=stopping_rounds scoring events (0 to disable)

Type: `int`, defaults to `0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"] = airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"] = airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8],
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> airlines_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(stopping_metric="auc",
...                                         stopping_rounds=3,
...                                         stopping_tolerance=1e-2,
...                                         seed=1234)
>>> airlines_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                    y=response,
...                    training_frame=train,
...                    validation_frame=valid)
>>> airlines_drf.auc(valid=True)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.stopping_metric
documentation:
Metric to use for early stopping (AUTO: logloss for classification, deviance for regression and anomaly_score
for Isolation Forest). Note that custom and custom_increasing can only be used in GBM and DRF with the Python
client.

Type: `Literal["auto", "deviance", "logloss", "mse", "rmse", "mae", "rmsle", "auc", "aucpr", "lift_top_group",
"misclassification", "mean_per_class_error", "custom", "custom_increasing"]`, defaults to `"auto"`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"] = airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"] = airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8],
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> airlines_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(stopping_metric="auc",
...                                         stopping_rounds=3,
...                                         stopping_tolerance=1e-2,
...                                         seed=1234)
>>> airlines_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                    y=response,
...                    training_frame=train,
...                    validation_frame=valid)
>>> airlines_drf.auc(valid=True)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.stopping_tolerance
documentation:
Relative tolerance for metric-based stopping criterion (stop if relative improvement is not at least this much)

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.001`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"] = airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"] = airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8],
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> airlines_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(stopping_metric="auc",
...                                         stopping_rounds=3,
...                                         stopping_tolerance=1e-2,
...                                         seed=1234)
>>> airlines_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                    y=response,
...                    training_frame=train,
...                    validation_frame=valid)
>>> airlines_drf.auc(valid=True)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.max_runtime_secs
documentation:
Maximum allowed runtime in seconds for model training. Use 0 to disable.

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.0`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars["economy_20mpg"] = cars["economy_20mpg"].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","acceleration","year"]
>>> response = "economy_20mpg"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(max_runtime_secs=10,
...                                     ntrees=10000,
...                                     max_depth=10,
...                                     seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_drf.auc(valid = True)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.seed
documentation:
Seed for pseudo random number generator (if applicable)

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"] = airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"] = airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> drf_w_seed_1 = H2ORandomForestEstimator(seed=1234)
>>> drf_w_seed_1.train(x=predictors,
...                    y=response,
...                    training_frame=train,
...                    validation_frame=valid)
>>> print('auc for the 1st model build with a seed:',
...        drf_w_seed_1.auc(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.build_tree_one_node
documentation:
Run on one node only; no network overhead but fewer cpus used. Suitable for small datasets.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars["economy_20mpg"] = cars["economy_20mpg"].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","acceleration","year"]
>>> response = "economy_20mpg"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(build_tree_one_node=True,
...                                     seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> cars_drf.auc(valid=True)
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.mtries
documentation:
Number of variables randomly sampled as candidates at each split. If set to -1, defaults to sqrt\{p} for
classification and p/3 for regression (where p is the # of predictors

Type: `int`, defaults to `-1`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> covtype = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/covtype/covtype.20k.data")
>>> covtype[54] = covtype[54].asfactor()
>>> predictors = covtype.columns[0:54]
>>> response = 'C55'
>>> train, valid = covtype.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> cov_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(mtries=30, seed=1234)
>>> cov_drf.train(x=predictors,
...               y=response,
...               training_frame=train,
...               validation_frame=valid)
>>> print('logloss', cov_drf.logloss(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.sample_rate
documentation:
Row sample rate per tree (from 0.0 to 1.0)

Type: `float`, defaults to `0.632`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> airlines= h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/airlines/allyears2k_headers.zip")
>>> airlines["Year"] = airlines["Year"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Month"] = airlines["Month"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["DayOfWeek"] = airlines["DayOfWeek"].asfactor()
>>> airlines["Cancelled"] = airlines["Cancelled"].asfactor()
>>> airlines['FlightNum'] = airlines['FlightNum'].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["Origin", "Dest", "Year", "UniqueCarrier",
...               "DayOfWeek", "Month", "Distance", "FlightNum"]
>>> response = "IsDepDelayed"
>>> train, valid= airlines.split_frame(ratios=[.8],
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> airlines_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(sample_rate=.7,
...                                         seed=1234)
>>> airlines_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                    y=response,
...                    training_frame=train,
...                    validation_frame=valid)
>>> print(airlines_drf.auc(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.sample_rate_per_class
documentation:
A list of row sample rates per class (relative fraction for each class, from 0.0 to 1.0), for each tree

Type: `List[float]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> covtype = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/covtype/covtype.20k.data")
>>> covtype[54] = covtype[54].asfactor()
>>> predictors = covtype.columns[0:54]
>>> response = 'C55'
>>> train, valid = covtype.split_frame(ratios=[.8],
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> print(train[response].table())
>>> rate_per_class_list = [1, .4, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1]
>>> cov_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(sample_rate_per_class=rate_per_class_list,
...                                    seed=1234)
>>> cov_drf.train(x=predictors,
...               y=response,
...               training_frame=train,
...               validation_frame=valid)
>>> print('logloss', cov_drf.logloss(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.binomial_double_trees
documentation:
For binary classification: Build 2x as many trees (one per class) - can lead to higher accuracy.

Type: `bool`, defaults to `False`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars["economy_20mpg"] = cars["economy_20mpg"].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","acceleration","year"]
>>> response = "economy_20mpg"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8], seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(binomial_double_trees=False,
...                                     seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> print('without binomial_double_trees:',
...        cars_drf.auc(valid=True))
>>> cars_drf_2 = H2ORandomForestEstimator(binomial_double_trees=True,
...                                       seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf_2.train(x=predictors,
...                  y=response,
...                  training_frame=train,
...                  validation_frame=valid)
>>> print('with binomial_double_trees:', cars_drf_2.auc(valid=True))
```

methods.name: h2o.estimators.random_forest.H2ORandomForestEstimator.checkpoint
documentation:
Model checkpoint to resume training with.

Type: `Union[None, str, H2OEstimator]`.

```
examples

```

```pycon
>>> cars = h2o.import_file("https://s3.amazonaws.com/h2o-public-test-data/smalldata/junit/cars_20mpg.csv")
>>> cars["economy_20mpg"] = cars["economy_20mpg"].asfactor()
>>> predictors = ["displacement","power","weight","acceleration","year"]
>>> response = "economy_20mpg"
>>> train, valid = cars.split_frame(ratios=[.8],
...                                 seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf = H2ORandomForestEstimator(ntrees=1,
...                                     seed=1234)
>>> cars_drf.train(x=predictors,
...                y=response,
...                training_frame=train,
...                validation_frame=valid)
>>> print(cars_drf.auc(valid=True))
```
